questions.question_text: A train travels $82.6$ km/hr. How many metres will it travel in $15$ minutes?
questions.solution:
Distance travelled in $1$ min = $\left(\frac{82.6}{60}\right)$ km.
$\therefore$ Distance travelled in $15$ min. = $\left(\frac{82.6}{60}\times15\right)$ km = $20.65$ km = $(20.65\times1000)$ m
= $20650$ m.

questions.question_text: How many minutes does Aditya take to cover a distance of $400$ m, if he runs at a speed of $20$ km/hr?
questions.solution:
Aditya's speed = $20$ km/hr = $\left(20\times\frac{5}{18}\right)$ m/sec = $\frac{50}{9}$ m/sec.
$\therefore$ Time taken to cover $400$ m = $\left(400\times\frac{9}{50}\right)$ sec = $72$ sec = $\frac{72}{60}$ min = $1\frac{1}{5}$ min.

questions.question_text: A cyclist covers a distance of $750$ m in $2$ min $30$ sec. What is the speed in km/hr of the cyclist?
questions.solution:
Speed = $\left(\frac{750}{150}\right)$ m/sec = $5$ m/sec = $\left(5\times\frac{18}{5}\right)$ km/hr = $18$ km/hr.
[$\because$ $2$ min $30$ sec = $150$ sec]

questions.question_text: A man walked at a speed of $4$ km/hr from point A to B and came back from point B to A at the speed of $6$ km/hr. What would be the ratio of the time taken by the man in walking from point A to B to that from point B to A?
questions.solution:
Ratio of speeds = $4 : 6 = 2 : 3$.
$\therefore$ Ratio of times taken = $\frac{1}{2} : \frac{1}{3} = 3 : 2$.

questions.question_text: A dog takes $4$ leaps for every $5$ leaps of a hare but $3$ leaps of a dog are equal to $4$ leaps of the hare. Compare their speeds.
questions.solution:
Let the distance covered in $1$ leap of the dog be $x$ and that covered in $1$ leap of the hare be $y$.
Then, $3x = 4y \Rightarrow x = \frac{4}{3}y \Rightarrow 4x = \frac{16}{3}y$.
$\therefore$ Ratio of speeds of dog and hare
= Ratio of distances covered by them in the same time
= $4x : 5y = \frac{16}{3}y : 5y = \frac{16}{3} : 5 = 16 : 15$.

questions.question_text: While covering a distance of $24$ km, a man noticed that after walking for $1$ hour and $40$ minutes, the distance covered by him was $\frac{5}{7}$ of the remaining distance. What was his speed in metres per second?
questions.solution:
Let the speed be $x$ km/hr.
Then, distance covered in $1$ hr. $40$ min. i.e., $1\frac{2}{3}$ hrs = $\frac{5x}{3}$ km.
Remaining distance = $\left(24 - \frac{5x}{3}\right)$ km.
$\therefore$ $\frac{5x}{3} = \frac{5}{7} \left(24 - \frac{5x}{3}\right) \Leftrightarrow \frac{5x}{3} = \frac{5}{7} \left(\frac{72 - 5x}{3}\right) \Leftrightarrow 7x = 72 - 5x$
$\Leftrightarrow 12x = 72 \Leftrightarrow x = 6$.
Hence, speed = $\left(6\times\frac{5}{18}\right)$ m/sec = $\frac{5}{3}$ m/sec = $1\frac{2}{3}$ m/sec.

questions.question_text: A trip to a destination is made in the following way : $900$ km by train at an average speed of $60$ km/hr, $3000$ km by plane at an average speed of $500$ km/hr, $400$ km by boat at an average speed of $25$ km/hr, $15$ km by taxi at an average speed of $45$ km/hr. What is the average speed for the entire journey?
questions.solution:
Total distance travelled = $(900 + 3000 + 400 + 15)$ km = $4315$ km.
Total time taken = $\left(\frac{900}{60} + \frac{3000}{500} + \frac{400}{25} + \frac{15}{45}\right)$ hr
= $\left(15 + 6 + 16 + \frac{1}{3}\right)$ hr = $37\frac{1}{3}$ hr = $\frac{112}{3}$ hr.
$\therefore$ Average speed for the whole journey
= $\left(4315\times\frac{3}{112}\right)$ km/hr = $115\frac{65}{112}$ km/hr.

questions.question_text: Peter can cover a certain distance in $1$ hr. $24$ min. by covering two-third of the distance at $4$ kmph and the rest at $5$ kmph. Find the total distance.
questions.solution:
Let the total distance be $x$ km. Then,
$\frac{2x/3}{4} + \frac{x/3}{5} = \frac{7}{5} \Leftrightarrow \frac{x}{6} + \frac{x}{15} = \frac{7}{5} \Leftrightarrow 7x = 42 \Leftrightarrow x = 6$.
$\therefore$ Total distance = $6$ km.

questions.question_text: One-third of a certain journey was covered at the speed of $20$ km/hr, one-fourth at $30$ km/hr and the rest at the speed of $50$ km/hr. Find the average speed per hour for the whole journey.
questions.solution:
Let the total distance be $x$ km. Then,
Distance covered at $20$ km/hr = $\frac{x}{3}$ km; Distance covered at $30$ km/hr = $\frac{x}{4}$ km;
Distance covered at $50$ km/hr = $\left[x - \left(\frac{x}{3} + \frac{x}{4}\right)\right]$ km = $\frac{5x}{12}$ km.
Total time taken = $\left[\frac{x/3}{20} + \frac{x/4}{30} + \frac{5x/12}{50}\right]$ hrs
= $\left(\frac{x}{60} + \frac{x}{120} + \frac{x}{120}\right)$ hrs = $\frac{4x}{120}$ hrs = $\frac{x}{30}$ hrs.
$\therefore$ Average speed for the whole journey = $\left(x\times\frac{30}{x}\right)$ km/hr = $30$ km/hr.

questions.question_text: A man travelled from the village to the post-office at the rate of $25$ kmph and walked back at the rate of $4$ kmph. If the whole journey took $5$ hours $48$ minutes, find the distance of the post-office from the village.
questions.solution:
Average speed = $\left(\frac{2xy}{x+y}\right)$ km/hr = $\left(\frac{2\times25\times4}{25+4}\right)$ km/hr = $\frac{200}{29}$ km/hr.
Distance travelled in $5$ hours $48$ minutes, i.e., $5\frac{4}{5}$ hrs = $\left(\frac{200}{29}\times\frac{29}{5}\right)$ km = $40$ km.
$\therefore$ Distance of the post-office from the village = $\left(\frac{40}{2}\right)$ km = $20$ km.

questions.question_text: An aeroplane flies along the four sides of a square at the speeds of $100, 200, 300$ and $400$ km/hr. Find the average speed of the plane around the field.
questions.solution:
Let each side of the square be $x$ km and let the average speed of the plane around the field be $y$ km/hr.
Then, $\frac{x}{100} + \frac{x}{200} + \frac{x}{300} + \frac{x}{400} = \frac{4x}{y} \Leftrightarrow \frac{25x}{1200} = \frac{4x}{y} \Leftrightarrow y = \left(\frac{1200\times4}{25}\right) = 192$.
$\therefore$ Average speed = $192$ km/hr.

questions.question_text: A fast train takes $3$ hours less than a slow train for a journey of $600$ km. If the speed of the slow train is $10$ km/hr less than that of the fast train, then find the speeds of the two trains.
questions.solution:
Let the speed of the fast train be $x$ km/hr.
Then, speed of the slow train = $(x - 10)$ km/hr.
$\therefore \frac{600}{(x-10)} - \frac{600}{x} = 3 \Leftrightarrow 600x - 600(x - 10) = 3x(x - 10)$
$\Leftrightarrow x^2 - 10x - 2000 = 0 \Leftrightarrow x^2 - 50x + 40x - 2000 = 0$
$\Leftrightarrow x(x - 50) + 40(x - 50) = 0 \Leftrightarrow (x - 50)(x + 40) = 0 \Leftrightarrow x = 50$.
Hence, speed of fast train = $50$ km/hr and speed of slow train = $40$ km/hr.

questions.question_text: By walking at $\frac{3}{4}$ of his usual speed, a man reaches his office $20$ minutes later than his usual time. Find the usual time taken by him to reach his office.
questions.solution:
New speed = $\frac{3}{4}$ of usual speed.
$\therefore$ New time taken = $\frac{4}{3}$ of usual time.
So, $\left(\frac{4}{3}\text{ of the usual time} - \text{usual time}\right) = 20$ min
$\Rightarrow \frac{1}{3}$ of the usual time = $20$ min $\Rightarrow$ Usual time = $60$ min = $1$ hr.

questions.question_text: A person reaches his destination $40$ minutes late if his speed is $3$ km/hr, and reaches $30$ minutes before time if his speed is $4$ km/hr. Find the distance of his destination from his starting point.
questions.solution:
Let the required distance be $x$ km.
Difference in the times taken at two speeds = $70$ min = $\frac{7}{6}$ hr.
$\therefore \frac{x}{3} - \frac{x}{4} = \frac{7}{6} \Leftrightarrow 4x - 3x = 14 \Leftrightarrow x = 14$.
Hence, the required distance is $14$ km.

questions.question_text: A carriage driving in a fog passed a man who was walking at the rate of $3$ kmph in the same direction. He could see the carriage for $4$ minutes and it was visible to him upto a distance of $100$ m. What was the speed of the carriage?
questions.solution:
Let the speed of the carriage be $x$ kmph. Then, relative speed = $(x - 3)$ kmph.
Distance covered in $4$ min i.e., $\frac{1}{15}$ hr at relative speed = $100$ m = $\frac{1}{10}$ km.
$\therefore (x - 3) = \frac{1}{10}\times15 = \frac{3}{2} = 1\frac{1}{2} \Leftrightarrow x = 3 + 1\frac{1}{2} = 4\frac{1}{2}$.
Hence, speed of the carriage = $4\frac{1}{2}$ kmph.

questions.question_text: A train after travelling $150$ km meets with an accident and then proceeds at $\frac{3}{5}$ of its former speed and arrives at its destination $8$ hours late. Had the accident occurred $360$ km further, it would have reached the destination $4$ hours late. What is the total distance travelled by the train?
questions.solution:
Let the original speed of the train be $x$ km/hr.
Then, $\frac{360}{(3/5)x} - \frac{360}{x} = (8 - 4) \Leftrightarrow \frac{600}{x} - \frac{360}{x} = 4 \Leftrightarrow \frac{240}{x} = 4 \Leftrightarrow x = 60$.
Let the total distance travelled by the train be $y$ km.
Then, $\left[\frac{150}{60} + \frac{(y - 150)}{(3/5)\times60}\right] - \frac{y}{60} = 8 \Leftrightarrow \left[\frac{150}{60} + \frac{5(y - 150)}{180}\right] - \frac{y}{60} = 8 \Leftrightarrow 2x = 1740 \Leftrightarrow x = 870$.
Hence, required distance = $870$ km.

questions.question_text: A ship $77$ km from the shore, springs a leak which admits $2\frac{1}{4}$ tonnes of water in $5\frac{1}{2}$ minutes. $92$ tonnes of water would sink it. But the pumps can throw out $12$ tonnes of water per hour. Find the average rate of sailing so that the ship may just reach the shore as it begins to sink.
questions.solution:
Quantity of water let in by the leak in $1$ min
= $\left(\frac{9/4}{11/2}\right)$ tonnes = $\left(\frac{9}{4}\times\frac{2}{11}\right)$ tonnes = $\frac{9}{22}$ tonnes.
Quantity of water thrown out by the pumps in $1$ min = $\frac{12}{60}$ tonnes = $\frac{1}{5}$ tonnes.
Net quantity of water filled in the ship in $1$ min = $\left(\frac{9}{22} - \frac{1}{5}\right)$ tonnes = $\frac{23}{110}$ tonnes.
$\frac{23}{110}$ tonnes water is filled in $1$ min.
$92$ tonnes water is filled in $\left(\frac{110}{23}\times92\right)$ min = $440$ min = $\frac{22}{3}$ hrs.

questions.question_text: Excluding the stoppages, the speed of a bus is 64 km/hr and including the stoppages, the speed of the bus is 48 km/hr. For how many minutes does the bus stop per hour?
questions.solution:
Due to stoppage, the bus covers $(64 - 48) = 16$ km less per hour.
Time taken to cover 16 km = $\left( \frac{16}{64} \times 60 \right)$ min = 15 min.
Hence, stoppage time per hour = 15 min.

questions.question_text: An aeroplane started 30 minutes later than the scheduled time from a place 1500 km away from its destination. To reach the destination at the scheduled time the pilot had to increase the speed by 250 km/hr. What was the speed of the aeroplane per hour during the journey?
questions.solution:
Let the original speed of the aeroplane be $x$ km/hr.
$\therefore \frac{1500}{x} - \frac{1500}{x + 250} = \frac{30}{60} = \frac{1}{2} \Leftrightarrow 3000(x + 250) - 3000x = x(x + 250)$
$\Leftrightarrow x^2 + 250x - 750000 = 0$
$\Leftrightarrow x^2 + 1000x - 750x - 750000 = 0$
$\Leftrightarrow x(x + 1000) - 750(x + 1000) = 0$
$\Leftrightarrow (x + 1000)(x - 750) = 0 \Leftrightarrow x = 750$.
Hence, speed of the aeroplane during the journey = $(750 + 250)$ km/hr = 1000 km/hr.

questions.question_text: Two boys A and B start at the same time to ride from Delhi to Meerut, 60 km away. A travels 4 km an hour slower than B. B reaches Meerut and at once turns back meeting A 12 km from Meerut. Find A’s speed.
questions.solution:
Let A’s speed = $x$ km/hr. Then, B’s speed = $(x + 4)$ km/hr.
Clearly, time taken by B to cover $(60 + 12)$, i.e., 72 km = time taken by A to cover $(60 - 12)$ i.e., 48 km
$\therefore \frac{72}{x + 4} = \frac{48}{x} \Leftrightarrow 72x = 48x + 192 \Leftrightarrow 24x = 192 \Leftrightarrow x = 8$.
Hence, A’s speed = 8 km/hr.

questions.question_text: A man covers a certain distance on a toy train. Had the train moved 4 km/hr faster, it would have taken 30 minutes less. If it moved 2 km/hr slower, it would have taken 20 minutes more. Find the distance.
questions.solution:
Let the distance be $x$ km and initial speed be $y$ km/hr.
Then, $\frac{x}{y} - \frac{x}{y + 4} = \frac{30}{60} = \frac{1}{2} \Leftrightarrow \frac{4x}{y(y + 4)} = \frac{1}{2} \Leftrightarrow 8x = y^2 + 4y \Leftrightarrow x = \frac{y^2 + 4y}{8} \dots(i)$
And, $\frac{x}{y - 2} - \frac{x}{y} = \frac{20}{60} = \frac{1}{3} \Leftrightarrow \frac{2x}{y(y - 2)} = \frac{1}{3} \Leftrightarrow 6x = y^2 - 2y \Leftrightarrow x = \frac{y^2 - 2y}{6} \dots(ii)$
From (i) and (ii), we have :
$\frac{y^2 + 4y}{8} = \frac{y^2 - 2y}{6} \Leftrightarrow 6y^2 + 24y = 8y^2 - 16y \Leftrightarrow 2y^2 = 40y \Leftrightarrow 2y = 40 \Leftrightarrow y = 20$.
Putting $y = 20$ in (i), we get : $x = 60$.
Hence, required distance = 60 km.

questions.question_text: A and B are two stations 390 km apart. A train starts from A at 10 a.m. and travels towards B at 65 kmph. Another train starts from B at 11 a.m. and travels towards A at 35 kmph. At what time do they meet?
questions.solution:
Suppose they meet $x$ hours after 10 a.m. Then,
(Distance moved by first in $x$ hrs) + [Distance moved by second in $(x - 1)$ hrs] = 390
$\therefore 65x + 35(x - 1) = 390 \Rightarrow 100x = 425 \Rightarrow x = 4\frac{1}{4}$.
So, they meet 4 hrs. 15 min. after 10 a.m., i.e., at 2.15 p.m.

questions.question_text: A goods train leaves a station at a certain time and at a fixed speed. After 6 hours, an express train leaves the same station and moves in the same direction at a uniform speed of 90 kmph. This train catches up the goods train in 4 hours. Find the speed of the goods train.
questions.solution:
Let the speed of the goods train be $x$ kmph.
Distance covered by goods train in 10 hours = Distance covered by express train in 4 hours
$\therefore 10x = 4 \times 90$ or $x = 36$.
So, speed of goods train = 36 kmph.

questions.question_text: A thief is spotted by a policeman from a distance of 100 metres. When the policeman starts the chase, the thief also starts running. If the speed of the thief be 8 km/hr and that of the policeman 10 km/hr, how far the thief will have run before he is overtaken?
questions.solution:
Relative speed of the policeman = $(10 - 8)$ km/hr = 2 km/hr.
Time taken by policeman to cover 100 m = $\left( \frac{100}{1000} \times \frac{1}{2} \right)$ hr = $\frac{1}{20}$ hr.
In $\frac{1}{20}$ hrs, the thief covers a distance of $\left( 8 \times \frac{1}{20} \right)$ km = $\frac{2}{5}$ km = 400 m.

questions.question_text: Two places A and B are 80 km apart from each other on a highway. A car starts from A and another from B at the same time. If they move in the same direction they meet each other in 8 hours. If they move in opposite directions towards each other, they meet in 1 hour 20 minutes. Determine the speeds of the cars.
questions.solution:
Let their speeds be $x$ kmph and $y$ kmph respectively.
Then, $\frac{80}{x - y} = 8 \Rightarrow x - y = 10 \dots(i)$
And, $\frac{80}{x + y} = 1\frac{20}{60} = \frac{4}{3} \Rightarrow x + y = 60 \dots(ii)$
Adding (i) and (ii), we get : $2x = 70$ or $x = 35$.
Putting $x = 35$ in (i), we get : $y = 25$.
Hence, the speeds of the two cars are 35 kmph and 25 kmph.

questions.question_text: A man takes 6 hours 30 min in going by a cycle and coming back by scooter. He would have lost 2 hours 10 min by going on cycle both ways. How long would it take him to go by scooter both ways?
questions.solution:
Let the distance be $x$ km. Then,
(Time taken to cover $x$ km by cycle) + (Time taken to cover $x$ km by scooter) = 6 hr 30 min
$\Rightarrow$ (Time taken to cover $2x$ km by cycle) + (Time taken to cover $2x$ km by scooter) = 13 hrs
But, time taken to cover $2x$ km by cycle = 8 hr 40 min.
$\therefore$ Time taken to cover $2x$ km by scooter = 13 hrs - 8 hr 40 min = 4 hr 20 min.
Hence, required time = 4 hr 20 min.

questions.question_text: Sneha is picked up by her father by car from college everyday. The college gets over at 4 p.m. daily. One day, the college got over an hour earlier than usual. Sneha started walking towards her house. Her father, unaware of this fact, leaves his house as usual, meets his daughter on the way, picks her up and they reach the house 15 minutes earlier than usual. What is the ratio of the father’s driving speed to Sneha’s walking speed?
questions.solution:
Since 15 minutes are saved, it means that Sneha’s father drives from the meeting point to the college and back to the meeting point in 15 min. i.e. he can drive from the meeting point to the college in $\frac{15}{2} = 7.5$ min.
But he reaches the college daily at 4 p.m. So Sneha and her father meet on the way at 3:52$\frac{1}{2}$ p.m.
Thus, Sneha walked for 52.5 min and covered the same distance as covered by her father in 7.5 min.
Since speed varies inversely as time taken to cover a distance, we have:
$\frac{\text{Father's driving speed}}{\text{Sneha's walking speed}} = \frac{52.5}{7.5} = \frac{7}{1}$.
Hence, required ratio = 7 : 1.

questions.question_text: A speed of 30.6 km/hr is the same as
questions.solution: $30.6\text{ km/hr} = \left(30.6\times\frac{5}{18}\right)\text{ m/sec} = \frac{153}{18}\text{ m/sec} = 8.5\text{ m/sec.}$

questions.question_text: A man riding his bicycle covers 150 metres in 25 seconds. What is his speed in km per hour?
questions.solution:
$\text{Speed} = \left(\frac{150}{25}\right)\text{ m/sec} = 6\text{ m/sec}$
$= \left(6\times\frac{18}{5}\right)\text{ km/hr} = \frac{108}{5}\text{ km/hr} = 21.6\text{ km/hr.}$

questions.question_text: A bus covers a distance of 2924 km in 43 hours. What is the speed of the bus?
questions.solution: $\text{Speed} = \left(\frac{2924}{43}\right)\text{ km/hr} = 68\text{ km/hr.}$

questions.question_text: A is travelling at 72 km per hour on a highway while B is travelling at a speed of 25 metres per second. What is the difference in their speeds in metres per second?
questions.solution:
$\text{A's speed} = 72\text{ km/hr} = \left(72\times\frac{5}{18}\right)\text{ m/sec} = 20\text{ m/sec.}$
$\text{B's speed} = 25\text{ m/sec.}$
$\text{Difference} = (25 - 20)\text{ m/sec} = 5\text{ m/sec.}$

questions.question_text: A motorist travelled between two towns, which are 65 km apart, in 2 hours and 10 minutes. Find the speed in metres per minute.
questions.solution:
$\text{Distance covered} = 65\text{ km} = 65000\text{ m.}$
$\text{Time taken} = 2\text{ hrs } 10\text{ min}$
$= [(2\times60) + 10]\text{ min} = 130\text{ min.}$
$\therefore \text{Speed} = \left(\frac{65000}{130}\right)\text{ m/min} = 500\text{ m/min.}$

questions.question_text: In track meets both 100 yards and 100 metres are used as distances. By how many metres is 100 metres longer than 100 yards?
questions.solution:
$1\text{ yard} = 0.9144\text{ m} \Rightarrow 100\text{ yards} = (100\times0.9144)\text{ m} = 91.44\text{ m.}$
$\therefore \text{Required difference} = (100 - 91.44)\text{ m} = 8.56\text{ m.}$

questions.question_text: Which of the following trains is the fastest?
questions.solution:
$25\text{ m/sec} = \left(25\times\frac{18}{5}\right)\text{ km/hr} = 90\text{ km/hr.}$
$\text{And, } 25\text{ m/sec} = (25\times60)\text{ m/min} = 1500\text{ m/min.}$
$\text{So, all the three speeds are equal.}$

questions.question_text: A person crosses a 600 m long street in 5 minutes. What is his speed in km per hour?
questions.solution: $\text{Speed} = \left(\frac{600}{5\times60}\right)\text{ m/sec} = 2\text{ m/sec}$

questions.question_text: A car covers a distance of 432 km at the speed of 48 km/hr. In how many hours will the car cover this distance?
questions.solution: $\text{Required time} = \left(\frac{432}{48}\right)\text{ hours} = 9\text{ hours.}$

questions.question_text: A man covered a distance of 180 km in 4 hours on a bike. How much distance will be cover on a bicycle in 8 hours if he rides the bicycle at one-sixth the speed of the bike?
questions.solution:
$\text{Speed of the bike} = \left(\frac{180}{4}\right)\text{ km/hr} = 45\text{ km/hr.}$
$\text{Speed of the bicycle} = \left(\frac{1}{6}\times45\right)\text{ km/hr} = 7.5\text{ km/hr.}$
$\therefore \text{Required distance} = (7.5\times8)\text{ km} = 60\text{ km.}$

questions.question_text: The ratio of the speeds of a car, a train and a bus is $5 : 9 : 4$. The average speed of the car, the bus and the train is 72 km/hr. What is the average speed of the car and the train together?
questions.solution:
$\text{Let the speeds of the car, train and bus be } 5x, 9x \text{ and } 4x\text{ km/hr respectively.}$
$\text{Then, } \frac{5x+9x+4x}{3} = 72 \Leftrightarrow 6x = 72 \Leftrightarrow x = 12.$
$\therefore \text{Speed of the car} = 60\text{ km/hr ;}$
$\text{speed of the train} = 108\text{ km/hr.}$
$\text{Average speed of car and train} = \left(\frac{60+108}{2}\right)\text{ km/hr} = 84\text{ km/hr.}$

questions.question_text: Car A travels at the speed of 65 km/hr and reaches its destination in 8 hours. Car B travels at the speed of 70 km/hr and reaches its destination in 4 hours. What is the ratio of the distance covered by car A and car B respectively?
questions.solution: $\text{Required ratio} = (65\times8) : (70\times4) = 520 : 280 = 13 : 7.$

questions.question_text: The average speed of a bus is one-third of the speed of a train. The train covers 1125 km in 15 hours. How much distance will the bus cover in 36 minutes?
questions.solution:
$\text{Speed of the train} = \left(\frac{1125}{15}\right)\text{ km/hr} = 75\text{ km/hr.}$
$\text{Speed of the bus} = \left(\frac{1}{3}\times75\right)\text{ km/hr} = 25\text{ km/hr.}$
$\text{Distance covered by the bus in } 60\text{ min} = 25\text{ km.}$
$\text{Distance covered by the bus in } 36\text{ min} = \left(\frac{25}{60}\times36\right)\text{ km} = 15\text{ km.}$

questions.question_text: The mileage of a motorbike A and a motorbike B is 42 km per litre and 52 km per litre respectively. Motorbike A covered 294 km and motorbike B covered 208 km. If the cost of 1 litre of petrol is ₹ 48, how much amount would be spent on petrol to cover the total distance by both the motor bikes together?
questions.solution:
$\text{Quantity of petrol consumed by both the motorbikes} = \left(\frac{294}{42} + \frac{208}{52}\right)\text{ litres} = 11\text{ litres.}$
$\therefore \text{Total amount spent on petrol} = \text{Rs } (48\times11) = \text{Rs } 528.$

questions.question_text: A train leaves Delhi at 4.10 P.M. and reaches Aligarh at 7.25 P.M. The average speed of the train is 40 km/hr. What is the distance from Delhi to Aligarh?
questions.solution:
$\text{Time taken} = 3\text{ hrs } 15\text{ min} = 3\frac{1}{4}\text{ hrs} = \frac{13}{4}\text{ hrs.}$
$\therefore \text{Required distance} = \left(40\times\frac{13}{4}\right)\text{ km} = 130\text{ km.}$

questions.question_text: A is 10 miles west of B. C is 30 miles north of B. D is 20 miles east of C. What is the distance from A to D?
questions.solution:
$\text{Required distance} = AD = \sqrt{(AE)^2 + (DE)^2}$
$= \sqrt{(30)^2 + (30)^2}$
$= \sqrt{900 + 900} = \sqrt{1800}$
$= 30\sqrt{2}\text{ miles.}$

questions.question_text: A plane flying north at 500 mph passes over a city at 12 noon. A plane flying east at the same altitude passes over the same city at 12 : 30 P.M. The plane is flying east at 400 mph. To the nearest hundred miles, how far apart are the two planes at 2 P.M.?
questions.solution:
$\text{Distance covered by the first plane till 2 P.M., i.e., in } 2\text{ hrs} = (500\times2)\text{ miles} = 1000\text{ miles.}$
$\text{Distance covered by the second plane till 2 P.M., i.e., in } 1\frac{1}{2}\text{ hrs} = \left(400\times\frac{3}{2}\right)\text{ miles} = 600\text{ miles.}$
$\therefore \text{Required distance} = AB = \sqrt{(1000)^2 + (600)^2}$
$= \sqrt{1000000 + 360000}$
$= \sqrt{1360000}\text{ miles} = 200\sqrt{34}\text{ miles}$
$= 1166\text{ miles } \approx 1200\text{ miles.}$

questions.question_text: A train travels at the speed of 65 km/hr and halts at 8 junctions for a certain time. It covers a distance of 1300 km in 1 day (24 hours). How long does the train stop at each junction, if it halts for the same period of time at all the junctions?
questions.solution:
$\text{Time taken to cover } 1300\text{ km} = \left(\frac{1300}{65}\right)\text{ hrs} = 20\text{ hrs.}$
$\text{Halt time} = (24 - 20)\text{ hrs} = 4\text{ hrs.}$
$\text{Halting time at each junction} = \left(\frac{4}{8}\times60\right)\text{ min} = 30\text{ min.}$

questions.question_text: Jane travelled $\frac{4}{7}$ as many miles on foot as by water and $\frac{2}{5}$ as many miles on horseback as by water. If she covered a total of 3036 miles, how many miles did she travel on foot?
questions.solution:
$\text{Suppose Jane travelled } x\text{ miles by water, } \frac{4}{7}x\text{ miles on foot and } \frac{2}{5}x\text{ miles on horseback.}$
$\text{Then, } x + \frac{4}{7}x + \frac{2}{5}x = 3036 \Leftrightarrow \frac{35x + 20x + 14x}{35} = 3036$
$\Leftrightarrow \frac{69x}{35} = 3036 \Leftrightarrow x = \left(\frac{3036\times35}{69}\right) = 1540.$
$\therefore \text{Distance travelled on foot} = \left(\frac{4}{7}\times1540\right)\text{ miles} = 880\text{ miles.}$

questions.question_text: A star is $8.1 \times 10^{13}$ km away from the earth. Suppose light travels at the speed of $3.0 \times 10^5$ km per second. How long will it take the light from the star to reach the earth?
questions.solution:
$\text{Required time} = \left(\frac{8.1\times10^{13}}{3.0\times10^5}\right)\text{ seconds} = 2.7\times10^8\text{ sec}$
$= \left(\frac{2.7\times10^8}{60\times60}\right)\text{ hrs} = 7.5\times10^4\text{ hrs.}$

questions.question_text: Akash leaves home for school which is 12 km from his house. After the school, he goes to his club which is 7 km from his school. If his house, school and club all fall in a line, then what is the minimum distance he has to travel to get back home?
questions.solution:
For the distance to be minimum, the club must lie between Akash’s home and school.
Hence, required distance = $(12 - 7)$ km = 5 km.

questions.question_text: A train covers a distance of $1\frac{1}{3}$ km in $1\frac{1}{4}$ hours with one stoppage of 10 minutes, two of 5 minutes and one of 3 minutes on the way. The average speed of the train is
questions.solution:
Actual time taken for the journey
$= 4$ hrs $15$ min $- (10 + 2 	imes 5 + 3)$ min
$= 4$ hrs $15$ min $- 23$ min $= 3$ hrs $52$ min $=  rac{26}{30}$ hrs $=  rac{116}{30}$ hrs.
$\therefore$ Average speed $=  rac{580}{3} 	imes  rac{30}{116}$ km/hr $= 50$ km/hr.

questions.question_text: Deepa rides her bike at an average speed of 30 km/hr and reaches her destination in 6 hours. Hema covers the same distance in 4 hours. If Deepa increases her average speed by 10 km/hr and Hema increases her average speed by 5 km/hr, what would be the difference in their time taken to reach the destination?
questions.solution:
Deepa’s original speed $= 30$ km/hr.
Deepa’s new speed $= (30 + 10)$ km/hr $= 40$ km/hr.
Distance $= (30 	imes 6)$ km $= 180$ km.
Hema’s original speed $=  rac{180}{4}$ km/hr $= 45$ km/hr.
Hema’s new speed
$= (45 + 5)$ km/hr $= 50$ km/hr.
Difference in time
$=  rac{180}{40} -  rac{180}{50}$ hrs $=  rac{9}{10}$ hrs
$=  rac{9}{10} 	imes 60$ min $= 54$ min.

questions.question_text: A monkey climbing up a pole ascends 6 metres and slips 3 metres in alternate minutes. If the pole is 60 metres high, how long will it take the monkey to reach the top?
questions.solution:
Net height ascended in $2$ min $= (6 - 3)$ m $= 3$ m.
Net height ascended in $36$ min $=  rac{3}{2} 	imes 36$ m $= 54$ m.
In the $37$th min, the monkey ascends $6$ m and reaches the top.
Hence, total time taken $= 37$ minutes.

questions.question_text: An aeroplane flies twice as fast as a train which covers 60 miles in 80 minutes. What distance will the aeroplane cover in 20 minutes?
questions.solution:
Time taken to cover $60$ miles $= 80$ min $=  rac{4}{3}$ hrs.
$\therefore$ Speed of the train $=  rac{60}{4/3}$ mph $= 45$ mph.
Speed of the aeroplane $= (2 	imes 45)$ mph $= 90$ mph.
Distance covered by the aeroplane in $60$ min $= 90$ miles.
Distance covered by the aeroplane in $20$ min
$=  rac{90}{60} 	imes 20$ miles $= 30$ miles.

questions.question_text: A boy is running at a speed of $p$ kmph to cover a distance of 1 km. But, due to the slippery ground, his speed is reduced by $q$ kmph ($p > q$). If he takes $r$ hours to cover the distance, then
questions.solution: Speed $=  rac{	ext{Distance}}{	ext{Time}} \Rightarrow p - q =  rac{1}{r}$.

questions.question_text: Ravi can walk a certain distance in 40 days when he rests 9 hours a day. How long will he take to walk twice the distance, twice as fast and rest twice as long each day?
questions.solution:
Let the required number of days be $x$.
More distance, More days required (Direct Proportion)
More speed, Less days required (Indirect Proportion)
More resting hours, More days required (Direct Proportion)
Distance $1 : 2$
Speed $2 : 1$
Resting hours $9 : 18$
$\therefore 1 	imes 2 	imes 9 	imes x = 2 	imes 1 	imes 18 	imes 40 \Leftrightarrow x =  rac{2 	imes 18 	imes 40}{2 	imes 9} = 80$.

questions.question_text: A car is driven at the speed of 100 km/hr and stops for 10 minutes at the end of every 150 km. To cover a distance of 1000 km, it will take
questions.solution:
Time taken to cover $150$ km $= (1$ hr $30$ min $+ 10$ min)
$= 1$ hr $40$ min $=  rac{25}{3}$ hr $=  rac{5}{3}$ hr.
Time taken to cover $(150 	imes 6)$ i.e., $900$ km
$=  rac{5}{3} 	imes 6$ hrs $= 10$ hrs.
Remaining $100$ km is covered in $1$ hour.
Total time taken $= (10 + 1)$ hrs $= 11$ hrs.

questions.question_text: A man takes 50 minutes to cover a certain distance at a speed of 6 km/hr. If he walks with a speed of 10 km/hr, he covers the same distance in
questions.solution:
Distance $= 	ext{Speed} 	imes 	ext{Time} =  rac{50}{60} 	imes 6$ km $= 5$ km.
$\therefore$ Required time $=  rac{	ext{Distance}}{	ext{Speed}} =  rac{5}{10}$ hrs $=  rac{1}{2}$ hr $= 30$ min.

questions.question_text: A truck covers a distance of 550 metres in 1 minute whereas a bus covers a distance of 33 kms in 45 minutes. The ratio of their speeds is
questions.solution: Ratio of speeds $=  rac{550}{60} 	imes  rac{18}{5} :  rac{33}{45} 	imes 60 = 33 : 44 = 3 : 4$.

questions.question_text: The ratio between the speeds of two trains is $7 : 8$. If the second train runs 400 kms in 4 hours, then the speed of the first train is
questions.solution:
Let the speeds of two trains be $7x$ and $8x$ km/hr.
Then, $ rac{400}{48} = 100 \Rightarrow x =  rac{100}{8} = 12.5$.
$\therefore$ Speed of first train $= (7 	imes 12.5)$ km/hr $= 87.5$ km/hr.

questions.question_text: A train travels at an average of 50 miles per hour for $1\frac{1}{2}$ hours and then travels at a speed of 70 miles per hour for $1\frac{1}{2}$ hours. How far did the train travel in the entire 4 hours?
questions.solution:
Total distance travelled $= \left[ \left( 50 	imes  rac{1}{2} \right) + \left( 70 	imes  rac{1}{2} \right) \right]$
miles $= (125 + 105)$ miles $= 230$ miles.

questions.question_text: A man in a train notices that he can count 21 telephone posts in one minute. If they are known to be 50 metres apart, then at what speed is the train travelling?
questions.solution:
Number of gaps between $21$ telephone posts $= 20$.
Distance travelled in $1$ minute $= (50 	imes 20)$ m $= 1000$ m $= 1$ km.
$\therefore$ Speed $= 60$ km/hr.

questions.question_text: Sound is said to travel in air at about 1100 feet per second. A man hears the axe striking the tree, $\frac{11}{5}$ seconds after he sees it strike the tree. How far is the man from the wood chopper?
questions.solution: Distance $=  rac{11}{5} 	imes 1100$ feet $= 2420$ feet.

questions.question_text: An express train travelled at an average speed of 100 km/hr, stopping for 3 minutes after every 75 km. How long did it take to reach its destination 600 km from the starting point?
questions.solution:
Time taken to cover $600$ km $=  rac{600}{100}$ hrs $= 6$ hrs.
Number of stoppages $= (600 \div 75) - 1 = 7$.
Total time of stoppage $= (3 	imes 7)$ min $= 21$ min.
Hence, total time taken $= 6$ hrs $21$ min.

questions.question_text: A certain distance is covered by a cyclist at a certain speed. If a jogger covers half the distance in double the time, the ratio of the speed of the jogger to that of the cyclist is :
questions.solution:
Let the distance covered by the cyclist be $x$ and the time taken be $y$. Then,
Required ratio $=  rac{1/2}{1/4} = 1 : 1 = 1 : 4$.

questions.question_text: A motor car starts with the speed of 70 km/hr with its speed increasing every two hours by 10 kmph. In how many hours will it cover 345 kms?
questions.solution:
Distance covered in first $2$ hours $= (70 	imes 2)$ km $= 140$ km.
Distance covered in next $2$ hours $= (80 	imes 2)$ km $= 160$ km.
Remaining distance $= 345 - (140 + 160) = 45$ km.
Speed in the fifth hour $= 90$ km/hr.
Time taken to cover $45$ km $=  rac{45}{90}$ hr $=  rac{1}{2}$ hr.
$\therefore$ Total time taken $= (2 + 2 +  rac{1}{2}) = 4\frac{1}{2}$ hrs.

questions.question_text: A bus moving at a speed of 24 m/s begins to slow at a rate of 3 m/s each second. How far does it go before stopping?
questions.solution:
This is a question on uniform retardation (as it is given that the car slows down at a fixed rate)
If $v$ is the final velocity, $u$ is the initial velocity, $a$ is the uniform acceleration (or retardation), $t$ is the time and $s$ is the distance covered, we have :
$v = u + at$ and $s = ut + \frac{1}{2}at^2$
Here, $v = 0, u = 24$ m/s, $a = -3$ m/s$^2$
$\therefore 0 = 24 - 3t \Rightarrow 3t = 24 \Rightarrow t = 8$.
And, $s = [24 	imes 8 + \frac{1}{2} 	imes (-3) 	imes 8^2]$ m $= (192 - 96)$ m $= 96$ m.

questions.question_text: A boy goes three equal distances, each of length $x$ km, with a speed of $y$ km/hr, $\frac{3}{5}y$ km/hr and $\frac{2}{5}y$ km/hr respectively. If the total time taken is 1 hour, then $x : y$ is equal to
questions.solution:
Total time taken
$= \left[ \frac{x}{y} + \frac{x}{3y} + \frac{x}{2y} \right]$ hours
$= \left[ \frac{6x + 2x + 3x}{6y} \right]$ hours $= \frac{31x}{6y}$ hours.
$\therefore \frac{31x}{6y} = 1 \Rightarrow \frac{x}{y} = \frac{6}{31}$.

questions.question_text: A long distance runner runs 9 laps of a 400 metres track everyday. His timings (in min) for four consecutive days are 88, 96, 89 and 87 respectively. On an average, how many metres/minute does the runner cover?
questions.solution:
Average speed $= \frac{\text{Total distance covered}}{\text{Total time taken}}$
$= \frac{4 \times 9 \times 400}{88 + 96 + 89 + 87}$ m/min $= \frac{14400}{360}$ m/min
$= 40$ m/min.

questions.question_text: An express train travelled at an average speed of 100 kmph, stopping for 3 minutes after 75 km. A local train travelled at a speed of 50 kmph, stopping for 1 minute after every 25 km. If the trains began travelling at the same time, how many kilometres did the local train travel in the time it took the express train to travel 600 km?
questions.solution:
Time taken by the express train to cover 600 km
$=  rac{600}{100}$ hrs = 6 hrs.
Number of stoppages = $(600 	ext{ ÷ } 75) - 1 = 7$.
Duration of stoppage = $(3 	imes 7)$ min = 21 min.
Total time taken = 6 hrs 21 min.
Total time taken by local train to cover 50 km (with stoppages) = 1 hr 2 min.
So, the local train covers $(50 	imes 6) = 300$ km in 6 hr 12 min.
In remaining 9 min, it covers
$ rac{50}{60} 	imes 9$ km = 7.5 km.
$\therefore$ Required distance = $(300 + 7.5)$ km = 307.5 km.

questions.question_text: A car starts running with the initial speed of 40 kmph, with its speed increasing every hour by 5 kmph. How many hours will it take to cover a distance of 385 km?
questions.solution:
Let the required number of hours be n.
Clearly, the car covers 40 km in first hour, 45 km in the second hour, 50 km in the third hour, and so on.
Thus, we have :
$40 + 45 + 50 + 	ext{.........}$ upto n terms = 385.
This is an A.P. with first term $a = 40$, common difference $d = 5$.
$\therefore S_n =  rac{n}{2} [2 	imes 40 + (n - 1) 5]$
So, $ rac{n}{2} [80 + 5(n - 1)] = 385 	ext{ } \Leftrightarrow 	ext{ } 80n + 5n^2 - 5n = 770 	ext{ } \Leftrightarrow$
$5n^2 + 75n - 770 = 0$
$\Leftrightarrow n^2 + 15n - 154 = 0 	ext{ } \Leftrightarrow 	ext{ } n^2 + 22n - 7n - 154 = 0$
$\Leftrightarrow n(n + 22) - 7 (n + 22) = 0 	ext{ } \Leftrightarrow 	ext{ } (n + 22) (n - 7) = 0 	ext{ } \Leftrightarrow 	ext{ } n = 7$.
Hence, required number of hours = 7.

questions.question_text: The speed of a car increases by 2 kms after every one hour. If the distance travelled in the first one hour was 35 kms, what was the total distance travelled in 12 hours?
questions.solution:
Total distance travelled in 12 hours = $(35 + 37 + 39 + 	ext{....}$ upto 12 terms).
This is an A.P. with first term, $a = 35$, number of terms, $n = 12$, common difference, $d = 2$.
$\therefore$ Required distance
$=  rac{12}{2} [2 	imes 35 + (12 - 1) 	imes 2] = 6(70 + 22) = 552$ km.

questions.question_text: A bus started its journey from Ramgarh and reached Devgarh in 44 minutes at its average speed of 50 km/hr. If the average speed of the bus is increased by 5 km/hr, how much time will it take to cover the same distance?
questions.solution:
Time taken to cover a distance is inversely proportional to the speed.
Let the required time be x min.
Then, $50 : 55 : : x : 44 	ext{ } \Leftrightarrow 	ext{ } 55x = 50 	imes 44$
$\Leftrightarrow x =  rac{50 	imes 44}{55}$ min = 40 min.

questions.question_text: The speeds of three cars are in the ratio $2 : 3 : 4$. The ratio of the times taken by these cars to travel the same distance is
questions.solution:
Ratio of speeds = $2 : 3 : 4$.
$\therefore$ Ratio of times taken = $ rac{1}{2} :  rac{1}{3} :  rac{1}{4} = 6 : 4 : 3$.

questions.question_text: The speeds of A and B are in the ratio $3 : 4$. A takes 20 minutes more than B to reach a destination. In what time does A reach the destination?
questions.solution:
Ratio of speeds = $3 : 4$.
Ratio of times taken = $ rac{1}{3} :  rac{1}{4} = 4 : 3$.
Let A and B take 4x and 3x minutes respectively to reach a destination.
Then, $4x - 3x = 20 	ext{ } \Leftrightarrow 	ext{ } x = 20$.
$\therefore$ Time taken by A = $4x = (4 	imes 20)$ min = 80 min = $1  rac{1}{3}$ hr.

questions.question_text: The speed of electric train is 25% more than that of steam engine train. What is the time taken by an electric train to cover a distance which a steam engine takes 4 hours 25 minutes to cover?
questions.solution:
Let the speed of steam engine train be x.
Then, speed of electric train = 125% of $x =  rac{5}{4}x$.
Time taken by steam engine = 4 hr 25 min = $4  rac{25}{60}$ hr = $4  rac{5}{12}$ hr = $ rac{53}{12}$ hr.
Let the time taken by electric train be t hours.
Then, $x :  rac{5}{4}x : : t :  rac{53}{12} 	ext{ } \Leftrightarrow 	ext{ }  rac{5}{4}t =  rac{53}{12}$
$\Leftrightarrow t =  rac{53}{12} 	imes  rac{4}{5} =  rac{53}{15} = 3  rac{8}{15}$ hr.

questions.question_text: A takes 2 hours more than B to walk $d$ km, but if A doubles his speed, then he can make it in 1 hour less than B. How much time does B require for walking $d$ km?
questions.solution:
Suppose B takes x hours to walk d km.
Then, A takes $(x + 2)$ hours to walk d km.
A's speed = $ rac{d}{x+2}$ km/hr and B's speed = $ rac{d}{x}$ km/hr.
A's new speed = $ rac{d}{x+2}$ km/hr.
$\therefore  rac{d}{x} -  rac{d}{x+2} = 1$
$\Leftrightarrow  rac{2}{x(x+2)} = 1 	ext{ } \Leftrightarrow 	ext{ } x^2 + 2x - 8 = 0 	ext{ } \Leftrightarrow 	ext{ } (x + 4)(x - 2) = 0 	ext{ } \Leftrightarrow 	ext{ } x = 2$.
Wait, the solution provided in the text says:
$\frac{d}{x} -  rac{d}{x+2} = 1 	ext{ } \Leftrightarrow 	ext{ }  rac{d}{x} -  rac{d}{x+2} = 1 	ext{ } \Leftrightarrow 	ext{ } x - 2 = 2 	ext{ } \Leftrightarrow 	ext{ } x = 4$.

questions.question_text: A train covers a distance of 10 km in 12 minutes. If its speed is decreased by 5 km/hr, the time taken by it to cover the same distance will be
questions.solution:
Speed = $10 	imes  rac{60}{12}$ km/hr = 50 km/hr.
New speed = $(50 - 5)$ km/hr = 45 km/hr.
$\therefore$ Time taken = $ rac{10}{45} 	imes 60$ min = $13  rac{1}{3}$ min = 13 min 20 sec.

questions.question_text: Anna left for city A from city B at 5.20 a.m. She travelled at the speed of 80 km/hr for 2 hours 15 minutes. After that the speed was reduced to 60 km/hr. If the distance between two cities is 350 kms, at what time did Anna reach city A?
questions.solution:
Distance covered in 2 hrs 15 min, i.e.,
$2  rac{1}{4}$ hrs = $80 	imes  rac{9}{4}$ hrs = 180 hrs.
Time taken to cover remaining distance
$= ( rac{350 - 180}{60})$ hrs = $ rac{17}{6}$ hrs = $2  rac{5}{6}$ hrs = 2 hrs 50 min.
Total time taken = (2 hrs 15 min + 2 hrs 50 min) = 5 hrs 5 min.
So, Anna reached city A at 10.25 a.m.

questions.question_text: An aeroplane covers a certain distance at a speed of 240 kmph in 5 hours. To cover the same distance in $2\frac{1}{3}$ hours, it must travel at a speed of
questions.solution:
Distance = $(240 	imes 5)$ km = 1200 km.
$\therefore$ Required speed = $ rac{1200}{5/3}$ km/hr = 720 km/hr.

questions.question_text: A salesman travels a distance of 50 km in 2 hours and 30 minutes. How much faster, in kilometres per hour, on an average, must he travel to make such a trip in $\frac{5}{6}$ hour less time?
questions.solution:
Time required = (2 hrs 30 min - 50 min)
$= 1$ hr 40 min = $1  rac{2}{3}$ hrs.
$\therefore$ Required speed = $ rac{50}{5/3}$ km/hr = 30 km/hr.
Original speed = $ rac{50}{2.5}$ km/hr = 20 km/hr.
$\therefore$ Difference in speed = $(30 - 20)$ km/hr = 10 km/hr.

questions.question_text: A person has to cover a distance of 6 km in 45 minutes. If he covers one-half of the distance in two-thirds of the total time; to cover the remaining distance in the remaining time, his speed (in km/hr) must be
questions.solution:
Remaining distance = 3 km
and Remaining time = $1 	imes  rac{45}{60}$ min = 15 min = $ rac{1}{4}$ hour.
$\therefore$ Required speed = $(3 	imes 4)$ km/hr = 12 km/hr.

questions.question_text: A man performs $\frac{3}{5}$ of the total journey by rail, $\frac{7}{20}$ by bus and the remaining 6.5 km on foot. His total journey is
questions.solution:
Let the total journey be x km.
Then, $ rac{3x}{5} +  rac{7x}{20} + 6.5 = x 	ext{ } \Leftrightarrow 	ext{ } 12x + 7x + 20 	imes 6.5 = 20x 	ext{ } \Leftrightarrow 	ext{ } x = 130$ km.

questions.question_text: A person wishes to reach his destination 90 km away in 3 hours but for the first half of the journey his speed was 20 km/hr. His average speed for the rest of the journey should be
questions.solution: Time taken to travel 45 km = $ rac{45}{20}$ hr = $ rac{9}{4}$ hr = $2  rac{1}{4}$ hr = 2 hr 15 min.

questions.question_text: A train is scheduled to cover the distance between two stations 46 km apart in one hour. If it travels 25 km at a speed of 40 km/hr, find the speed for the remaining journey to complete it in the scheduled time.
questions.solution:
Time taken to travel 25 km = $ rac{25}{40}$ hr = $ rac{5}{8}$ hr.
Remaining time = $(1 -  rac{5}{8})$ hr = $ rac{3}{8}$ hr.
$\therefore$ Required speed = $21 	imes  rac{8}{3}$ km/hr = 56 km/hr.

questions.question_text: How long must a driver take to drive the final 70 miles of a trip if he wants to average 50 miles an hour for the entire trip and during the first part of the trip he drove 50 miles in $1\frac{1}{2}$ hours?
questions.solution:
Total distance = $(70 + 50)$ miles = 120 miles.
Average speed = 50 miles/hour.
Required time of journey = $ rac{120}{50}$ hr = $ rac{12}{5}$ hr = $2  rac{2}{5}$ hr = 2 hr 24 min.
Time taken to cover 50 miles
$= 1  rac{1}{2}$ hr = 1 hr 30 min.
$\therefore$ Remaining time = (2 hr 24 min - 1 hr 30 min) = 54 min.

questions.question_text: A can complete a journey in 10 hours. He travels first half of the journey at the rate of 21 km/hr and second half at the rate of 24 km/hr. Find the total journey in km.
questions.solution:
Let the total distance be x km. Then,
$ rac{x}{21} +  rac{x}{24} = 10 	ext{ } \Leftrightarrow 	ext{ }  rac{8x + 7x}{168} = 10 	ext{ } \Leftrightarrow 	ext{ } 15x = 168 	imes 10 	ext{ } \Leftrightarrow 	ext{ } x =  rac{168 	imes 10}{15} = 224$ km.

questions.question_text: A motorcyclist completes a certain journey in 5 hours. He covers one-third distance at 60 km/hr and the rest at 80 km/hr. The length of the journey is
questions.solution:
Let the length of the journey be x km. Then,
$ rac{x}{60} +  rac{x}{80} = 3 	ext{ } \Leftrightarrow 	ext{ }  rac{4x + 3x}{240} = 3 	ext{ } \Leftrightarrow 	ext{ } 7x = 720 	ext{ } \Leftrightarrow 	ext{ } x = 360$.

questions.question_text: A person travels equal distances with speeds of 3 km/hr, 4 km/hr and 5 km/hr and takes a total time of 47 minutes. The total distance (in km) is
questions.solution:
Let the total distance be 3x km.
Then, $ rac{x}{3} +  rac{x}{4} +  rac{x}{5} = 47 	ext{ } \Leftrightarrow 	ext{ }  rac{20x + 15x + 12x}{60} = 47 	ext{ } \Leftrightarrow 	ext{ }  rac{47x}{60} = 47 	ext{ } \Leftrightarrow 	ext{ } x = 1$.
$\therefore$ Total distance = $(3 	imes 1)$ km = 3 km.

questions.question_text: A person travels 285 km in 6 hours in two stages. In the first part of the journey, he travels by bus at the speed of 40 km per hour. In the second part of the journey, he travels by train at the speed of 55 km per hour. How much distance did he travel by train?
questions.solution:
Let the distance travelled by the train be $x$ km.
Then, distance travelled by bus = $(285 - x)$ km.
$\frac{x}{40} + \frac{285 - x}{55} = 6$
$\frac{11x + 8(285 - x)}{440} = 6$
$11x - 8x + 2280 = 2640$
$3x = 495$
$x = 165$.
Hence, distance travelled by train = $165$ km.

questions.question_text: A is faster than B. A and B each walk 24 km. The sum of their speeds is 7 km/hr and the sum of times taken by them is 14 hours. Then, A’s speed is equal to
questions.solution:
Let A's speed = $x$ km/hr. Then, B's speed = $(7 - x)$ km/hr.
So, $\frac{24}{x} + \frac{24}{7 - x} = 14$
$24(7 - x) + 24x = 14x(7 - x)$
$14x^2 - 98x + 168 = 0$
$x^2 - 7x + 12 = 0$
$(x - 3)(x - 4) = 0$
$x = 3$ or $x = 4$.
Since A is faster than B, so A's speed = $4$ km/hr and B's speed = $3$ km/hr.

questions.question_text: A person travels from P to Q at a speed of 40 kmph and returns by increasing his speed by 50%. What is his average speed for both the trips?
questions.solution:
Speed on return trip = $150\%$ of $40 = 60$ kmph.
Average speed = $\frac{2 \times 40 \times 60}{40 + 60} = \frac{4800}{100} = 48$ km/hr.

questions.question_text: An aeroplane flies from place A to place B at the speed of 500 km/hr. On the return journey, its speed is 700 km/hr. The average speed of the aeroplane for the entire journey is
questions.solution: Average speed = $\frac{2 \times 500 \times 700}{500 + 700} = \frac{1750}{3} = 583\frac{1}{3}$ km/hr.

questions.question_text: A car covers a distance from Town I to Town II at the speed of 56 km/hr and from Town II to Town I at the speed of 53 km/hr. What is the average speed of the car?
questions.solution: Average speed = $\frac{2 \times 56 \times 53}{56 + 53} = \frac{5936}{109} = 54.45$ km/hr $\approx 54.5$ km/hr.

questions.question_text: A man can walk uphill at the rate of $2\frac{1}{2}$ km/hr and downhill at the rate of $3\frac{1}{4}$ km/hr. If the total time required to walk a certain distance up the hill and return to the starting point was 4 hr 36 min, then what was the distance walked up the hill by the man?
questions.solution:
Average speed = $\frac{2 \times 5 \times 13}{5 + 13} = \frac{65}{4}$ km/hr.
Total time taken = $4$ hr $36$ min = $4\frac{36}{60} = 4\frac{3}{5} = \frac{23}{5}$ hr.
Total distance covered uphill and downhill = $\frac{65}{23} \times \frac{23}{5} = 13$ km.
Distance walked uphill = $\frac{13}{2} = 6\frac{1}{2}$ km.

questions.question_text: A man drives 150 km to the seashore in 3 hours 20 min. He returns from the shore to the starting point in 4 hours 10 min. Let r be the average rate for the entire trip. Then the average rate for the trip going exceeds r, in kilometres per hour, by
questions.solution:
Time taken to cover $150$ km in going trip = $3$ hr $20$ min = $3\frac{20}{60} = 3\frac{1}{3} = \frac{10}{3}$ hr.
Speed in going trip = $150 \times \frac{3}{10} = 45$ km/hr.
Time taken to cover $150$ km in return trip = $4$ hr $10$ min = $4\frac{1}{6} = \frac{25}{6}$ hr.
Speed in return trip = $150 \times \frac{6}{25} = 36$ km/hr.
Average speed = $\frac{2 \times 45 \times 36}{45 + 36} = 40$ km/hr.
Required difference = $(45 - 40) = 5$ km/hr.

questions.question_text: The average speed of a train in the onward journey is 25% more than that in the return journey. The train halts for one hour on reaching the destination. The total time taken for the complete to and fro journey is 17 hours, covering a distance of 800 km. The speed of the train in the onward journey is
questions.solution:
Let the speed in return journey be $x$ km/hr.
Then, speed in onward journey = $\frac{125}{100}x = \frac{5}{4}x$.
Average speed = $\frac{2 \times \frac{5}{4}x \times x}{\frac{5}{4}x + x} = \frac{10}{9}x$.
$\frac{9}{10} \times 800 = 16x \Rightarrow x = 45$.
So, speed in onward journey = $\frac{5}{4} \times 45 = 56.25$ km/hr.

questions.question_text: I started on my bicycle at 7 a.m. to reach a certain place. After going a certain distance, my bicycle went out of order. Consequently, I rested for 35 minutes and came back to my house walking all the way. I reached my house at 1 p.m. If my cycling speed is 10 kmph and my walking speed is 1 kmph, then on my bicycle I covered a distance of
questions.solution:
Time taken = $5$ hrs $25$ min = $\frac{65}{12}$ hrs.
Let the required distance be $x$ km.
$\frac{x}{10} + \frac{x}{1} = \frac{65}{12} \Rightarrow x = \frac{325}{66} = 4\frac{61}{66}$ km.

questions.question_text: A, B and C are on a trip by a car. A drives during the first hour at an average speed of 50 km/hr. B drives during the next 2 hours at an average speed of 48 km/hr. C drives for the next 3 hours at an average speed of 52 km/hr. They reached their destination after exactly 6 hours. Their mean speed was :
questions.solution:
Total distance travelled = $(50 \times 1 + 48 \times 2 + 52 \times 3) = 302$ km.
Total time taken = $6$ hrs.
Mean speed = $\frac{302}{6} = 50\frac{1}{3}$ km/hr.

questions.question_text: A car covers the first 39 kms of its journey in 45 minutes and covers the remaining 25 km in 35 minutes. What is the average speed of the car?
questions.solution:
Total distance travelled = $(39 + 25) = 64$ km.
Total time taken = $(45 + 35) = 80$ min = $\frac{4}{3}$ hr.
Average speed = $64 \times \frac{3}{4} = 48$ km/hr.

questions.question_text: A train travels at a speed of 30 km/hr for 12 minutes and at a speed of 45 km/hr for the next 8 minutes. The average speed of the train for this journey is
questions.solution:
Total distance travelled = $(30 \times \frac{12}{60} + 45 \times \frac{8}{60}) = 12$ km.
Total time taken = $(12 + 8) = 20$ min = $\frac{1}{3}$ hr.
Average speed = $(12 \times 3) = 36$ km/hr.

questions.question_text: A man on tour travels 160 km by car at 64 km/hr and another 160 km by bus at 80 km/hr. The average speed for the whole journey is
questions.solution:
Total time taken = $\frac{160}{64} + \frac{160}{80} = \frac{9}{2}$ hrs.
Average speed = $320 \times \frac{2}{9} = 71.11$ km/hr.

questions.question_text: A boy rides his bicycle 10 km at an average speed of 12 km/hr and again travels 12 km at an average speed of 10 km/hr. His average speed for the entire trip is approximately
questions.solution:
Total distance travelled = $(10 + 12) = 22$ km.
Total time taken = $\frac{10}{12} + \frac{12}{10} = \frac{61}{30}$ hrs.
Average speed = $22 \times \frac{30}{61} = 10.8$ km/hr.

questions.question_text: A man travels 600 km by train at 80 km/hr, 800 km by ship at 40 km/hr, 500 km by aeroplane at 400 km/hr and 100 km by car at 50 km/hr. What is the average speed for the entire distance?
questions.solution:
Total distance travelled = $(600 + 800 + 500 + 100) = 2000$ km.
Total time taken = $\frac{600}{80} + \frac{800}{40} + \frac{500}{400} + \frac{100}{50} = \frac{123}{4}$ hrs.
Average speed = $2000 \times \frac{4}{123} = 65\frac{5}{123}$ km/hr.

questions.question_text: A cyclist rides 24 km at 16 kmph and further 36 km at 15 kmph. Find his average speed for the journey.
questions.solution:
Total distance travelled = $(24 + 36) = 60$ km.
Total time taken = $\frac{24}{16} + \frac{36}{15} = \frac{39}{10}$ hrs.
Average speed = $60 \times \frac{10}{39} = 15.38$ km/hr.

questions.question_text: A person travels three equal distances at a speed of x km/hr, y km/hr and z km/hr respectively. What is the average speed for the whole journey?
questions.solution:
Let each distance be equal to $d$. Total distance = $3d$.
Total time taken = $\frac{d}{x} + \frac{d}{y} + \frac{d}{z} = \frac{d(xy + yz + zx)}{xyz}$.
Average speed = $\frac{3d \times xyz}{d(xy + yz + zx)} = \frac{3xyz}{xy + yz + zx}$ km/hr.

questions.question_text: A car travels the first one-third of a certain distance with a speed of 10 km/hr, the next one-third distance with a speed of 20 km/hr, and the last one-third distance with a speed of 60 km/hr. The average speed of the car for the whole journey is
questions.solution:
Let the whole distance be $x$ km and average speed be $y$ km/hr.
$\frac{x/3}{10} + \frac{x/3}{20} + \frac{x/3}{60} = \frac{x}{y}$
$\frac{x}{30} + \frac{x}{60} + \frac{x}{180} = \frac{x}{y}$
$\frac{1}{18} = \frac{1}{y} \Rightarrow y = 18$ km/hr.

questions.question_text: A motorist covers a distance of 39 km in 45 minutes by moving at a speed of x kmph for the first 15 minutes, then moving at double the speed for the next 20 minutes and then again moving at his original speed for the rest of the journey. Then, x is equal to :
questions.solution:
$\frac{15x}{60} + \frac{20x}{60} + \frac{10x}{60} = 39$
$\frac{45x}{60} = 39 \Rightarrow \frac{3x}{4} = 39 \Rightarrow x = 52$. (Note: The solution in the book has a calculation error, $3x+8x+2x=468$ is not derived from the previous step correctly, but the result $x=36$ is stated.)

questions.question_text: Mary jogs 9 km at a speed of 6 km per hour. At what speed would she need to jog during the next 1.5 hours to have an average of 9 km per hour for the entire jogging session?
questions.solution:
Let speed of jogging be $x$ km/hr.
Total time taken = $\frac{9}{6} + 1.5 = 3$ hrs.
Total distance covered = $(9 + 1.5x)$ km.
$\frac{9 + 1.5x}{3} = 9 \Rightarrow 9 + 1.5x = 27 \Rightarrow 1.5x = 18 \Rightarrow x = 12$ kmph.

questions.question_text: A family, planning a weekend trip, decides to spend not more than a total of 8 hours driving. By leaving early in the morning, they can average 40 miles per hour on the way to their destination. Due to the heavy Sunday traffic, they average only 30 miles per hour on the return trip. What is the farthest distance from home they can plan to go?
questions.solution:
Ratio of speeds while going and returning = $40:30=4:3$.
Ratio of times taken while going and returning = $3:4$.
Time taken while going = $\left(\frac{3}{7}\times8\right)$ hr = $\frac{24}{7}$ hr.
Time taken while returning = $\left(\frac{4}{7}\times8\right)$ hr = $\frac{32}{7}$ hr.
$\therefore$ Required distance = $\left(40\times\frac{24}{7}\right)$ miles = $\frac{960}{7}$ miles
$=137.14$ miles.

questions.question_text: A car travelling with $\frac{5}{7}$ of its actual speed covers 42 km in 1 hr 40 min 48 sec. Find the actual speed of the car.
questions.solution:
Time taken = $1$ hr $40$ min $48$ sec
$=1$ hr $40\frac{4}{5}$ min = $1\frac{51}{75}$ hrs = $\frac{126}{75}$ hrs.
Let the actual speed be $x$ km/hr.
Then, $\left(\frac{5}{7}\times x\times\frac{126}{75}\right)=42$ or $x=\left(\frac{42\times7\times75}{5\times126}\right)=35$ km/hr.

questions.question_text: A train running at $\frac{7}{11}$ of its own speed reached a place in 22 hours. How much time could be saved if the train would have run at its own speed?
questions.solution:
New speed = $\frac{7}{11}$ of usual speed.
$\therefore$ New time = $\frac{11}{7}$ of usual time.
So, $\frac{11}{7}$ of usual time = $22$ hrs
$\Rightarrow$ usual time = $\left(22\times\frac{7}{11}\right)=14$ hrs.
Hence, time saved = $(22-14)=8$ hrs.

questions.question_text: A man can reach a certain place in 30 hours. If he reduces his speed by $\frac{1}{15}$th, he goes 10 km less in that time. Find his speed.
questions.solution:
Let the speed be $x$ km/hr.
Then, $30x-30\times\frac{14}{15}=10\Rightarrow2x=10\Rightarrow x=5$ km/hr.

questions.question_text: Walking $\frac{6}{7}$th of his usual speed, a man is 12 minutes too late. The usual time taken by him to cover that distance is
questions.solution:
New speed = $\frac{6}{7}$ of usual speed.
New time = $\frac{7}{6}$ of usual time
$\therefore \left(\frac{7}{6}\text{ of usual time}\right) - (\text{usual time}) = \frac{1}{5}$ hr.
$\Rightarrow \frac{1}{6}$ of usual time = $\frac{1}{5}$ hr
$\Rightarrow$ usual time = $\frac{6}{5}$ hr = $1$ hr $12$ min.

questions.question_text: The average speed of a train is 20% less on the return journey than on the onward journey. The train halts for half an hour at the destination station before starting on the return journey. If the total time taken for the to and fro journey is 23 hours, covering a distance of 1000 km, the speed of the train on the return journey is
questions.solution:
Let the average speed on the onward journey be $x$ km/hr.
Then, average speed on return journey
$= (80\% \text{ of } x)$ km/hr = $\left(\frac{4}{5}x\right)$ km/hr.
$\therefore \frac{500}{x} + \frac{500}{\left(\frac{4}{5}x\right)} = 23 \Rightarrow \frac{500}{x} + \frac{625}{x} = 45\times2$
$\Rightarrow \frac{1125}{x} = \frac{45}{2} \Rightarrow x = \frac{1125\times2}{45}=50$.
Hence, speed on return journey
$= \left(\frac{4}{5}\times50\right)$ km/hr = $40$ km/hr.

questions.question_text: A train increases its normal speed by 12.5% and reaches its destination 20 minutes earlier. What is the actual time taken by the train in the journey?
questions.solution:
Let the normal speed of the train be $x$ km/hr.
Then, new speed = $112\frac{1}{2}\%$ of $x$ km/hr
$= \left(\frac{225}{100}\times x\right)$ km/hr = $\left(\frac{9}{8}x\right)$ km/hr.
Let the distance covered be $d$ km.
Then, $\frac{d}{\left(\frac{9}{8}x\right)} - \frac{d}{x} = \frac{20}{60} = \frac{1}{3}$
$\Rightarrow \frac{8d}{9x} - \frac{d}{x} = \frac{1}{3}$
$\Rightarrow \frac{d}{9x} = \frac{1}{3} \Rightarrow d=3x$.
$\therefore$ Actual time taken = $\frac{d}{x} = \frac{3x}{x} = 3$ hours = $180$ min.

questions.question_text: A student walks from his house at a speed of $2\frac{1}{2}$ km per hour and reaches his school 6 minutes late. The next day he increases his speed by 1 km per hour and reaches 6 minutes before school time. How far is the school from his house?
questions.solution:
Let the distance be $x$ km.
Difference in timings = $12$ min = $\frac{12}{60}$ hr = $\frac{1}{5}$ hr.
$\therefore \left(\frac{x}{5} - \frac{x}{7}\right) = \frac{1}{5}$
$\Rightarrow \frac{2x}{35} = \frac{1}{5} \Rightarrow x = \frac{35}{10} = 3\frac{1}{4}$ km.

questions.question_text: With an average speed of 50 km/hr, a train reaches its destination in time. If it goes with an average speed of 40 km/hr, it is late by 24 minutes. The total journey is
questions.solution:
Difference between timings = $24$ min = $\frac{24}{60}$ hr = $\frac{2}{5}$ hr.
Let the length of the journey be $x$ km.
Then, $\frac{x}{40} - \frac{x}{50} = \frac{2}{5} \Rightarrow \frac{x}{200} = \frac{2}{5} \Rightarrow x = \left(\frac{2}{5}\times200\right) = 80$ km.

questions.question_text: If a train runs at 40 kmph, it reaches its destination late by 11 minutes but if it runs at 50 kmph, it is late by 5 minutes only. The correct time for the train to complete its journey is
questions.solution:
Let the correct time to complete the journey be $x$ min.
Distance covered in $(x+11)$ min. at $40$ kmph
= Distance covered in $(x+5)$ min. at $50$ kmph
$\therefore \frac{(x+11)}{60}\times40 = \frac{(x+5)}{60}\times50 \Rightarrow x = 19$ min.

questions.question_text: Robert is travelling on his cycle and has calculated to reach point A at 2 P.M. if he travels at 10 kmph; he will reach there at 12 noon if he travels at 15 kmph. At what speed must he travel to reach A at 1 P.M.?
questions.solution:
Let the distance travelled be $x$ km.
Then, $\frac{x}{10} - \frac{x}{15} = 2 \Rightarrow \frac{x}{30} = 2 \Rightarrow x = 60$ km.
Time taken to travel $60$ km at $10$ km/hr = $\frac{60}{10}$ hrs = $6$ hrs.
So, Robert started $6$ hours before $2$ P.M. i.e., at $8$ A.M.
$\therefore$ Required speed = $\frac{60}{5}$ kmph = $12$ kmph.

questions.question_text: Ravi walks to and fro to a shopping mall. He spends 30 minutes shopping. If he walks at a speed of 10 km an hour, he returns home at 19.00 hours. If he walks at 15 km an hour, he returns home at 18.30 hours. How far must he walk in order to return home at 18.15 hours?
questions.solution:
Let the to and fro distance to the mall be $x$ km.
Then, $\frac{x}{10} - \frac{x}{15} = \frac{30}{60} = \frac{1}{2} \Rightarrow \frac{x}{30} = \frac{1}{2} \Rightarrow x = 15$.
Time taken to travel $15$ km at $10$ km/hr
$= \frac{15}{10} = 1\frac{1}{2}$ hrs = $1\frac{1}{2}$ hrs.
Since $30$ minutes were spent in shopping, so Ravi started
for the mall $2$ hours before $19.00$ hrs i.e., at $17.00$ hrs.
Now, required time for to and fro journey
$= (18.15 \text{ hrs} - 17.00 \text{ hrs}) - 30 \text{ min} = 45 \text{ min} = \frac{3}{4}$ hrs.
Hence, required speed = $\left(15\times\frac{4}{3}\right)$ km/hr = $20$ km/hr.

questions.question_text: A person travels 285 km in 6 hours in two stages. In the first part of the journey, he travels by bus at the speed of 40 km/hr. In the second part of the journey, he travels by train at the speed of 55 km/hr. How much distance does he travel by train?
questions.solution:
Let the distance travelled by bus be $x$ km.
Then, distance travelled by train = $(285 - x)$ km.
$\therefore \frac{x}{40} + \frac{(285 - x)}{55} = 6 \Rightarrow \frac{11x + 8(285 - x)}{440} = 6$
$\Rightarrow 11x - 8x + 2280 = 2640 \Rightarrow 3x = 360 \Rightarrow x = 120$.
Hence, distance travelled by train = $(285 - 120)$ km = $165$ km.

questions.question_text: A man covered a certain distance at some speed. Had he moved 3 kmph faster, he would have taken 40 minutes less. If he had moved 2 kmph slower, he would have taken 40 minutes more. The distance (in km) is
questions.solution:
Let distance = $x$ km and usual rate = $y$ kmph.
$\frac{x}{y} - \frac{x}{y+3} = \frac{40}{60}$ or $2(y+3) = 9y$ ... (i)
And, $\frac{x}{y-2} - \frac{x}{y} = \frac{40}{60}$ or $3(y-2) = 2y$ ... (ii)
On dividing (i) by (ii), we get :
$2(y+3) = 3(y-2) \Rightarrow y = 12$.
$\therefore$ Distance = $x = \frac{2y(y+3)}{9} = \frac{2\times12\times15}{9}$ km = $40$ km.

questions.question_text: A train covered a certain distance at a uniform speed. If the train had been 6 km/hr faster, then it would have taken 4 hours less than the scheduled time. And, if the train were slower by 6 km/hr, then the train would have taken 6 hours more than the scheduled time. The length of the journey is
questions.solution:
Let distance = $x$ km and usual speed = $y$ kmph.
$\frac{x}{y} - \frac{x}{y+6} = 4 \Rightarrow 6x = 4y(y+6)$ ... (i)
And, $\frac{x}{y-6} - \frac{x}{y} = 6 \Rightarrow 6x = 6y(y-6)$ ... (ii)
From (i) and (ii), we get :
$4y(y+6) = 6y(y-6) \Rightarrow 2(y+6) = 3(y-6) \Rightarrow y = 30$.
$\therefore$ Length of the journey = $x = \frac{4y(y+6)}{6} = \frac{4\times30\times36}{6}$ km = $720$ km.

questions.question_text: A car travels from P to Q at a constant speed. If its speed were increased by 10 km/hr, it would have taken one hour lesser to cover the distance. It would have taken further 45 minutes lesser if the speed was further increased by 10 km/hr. What is the distance between the two cities?
questions.solution:
Let distance = $x$ km and usual rate = $y$ kmph.
Then, $\frac{x}{y} - \frac{x}{y+10} = 1$ or $x(y+10) = 10y^2$ ... (i)
And $\frac{x}{y} - \frac{x}{y+20} = \frac{7}{4}$ or $x(y+20) = \frac{80}{7}y^2$ ... (ii)
On dividing (i) by (ii), we get : $y = 60$.

questions.question_text: A train can travel 50% faster than a car. Both start from point A at the same time and reach point B 75 kms away from A at the same time. On the way, however, the train lost about 12.5 minutes while stopping at the stations. The speed of the car is
questions.solution:
Let speed of the car be $x$ kmph.
Then, speed of the train = $150\%$ of $x = \left(\frac{3}{2}x\right)$ kmph.
$\therefore \frac{75}{x} - \frac{75}{\left(\frac{3}{2}x\right)} = \frac{10}{60} \Rightarrow \frac{75}{x} - \frac{50}{x} = \frac{1}{6}$
$\Rightarrow \frac{25}{x} = \frac{1}{6} \Rightarrow x = 150$ kmph.
Speed of train = $\left(\frac{3}{2}\times150\right) = 225$ kmph.

questions.question_text: Excluding stoppages, the speed of a bus is 54 kmph and including stoppages, it is 45 kmph. For how many minutes does the bus stop per hour?
questions.solution:
Due to stoppages, it covers $9$ km less.
Time taken to cover $9$ km = $\left(\frac{9}{54}\times60\right)$ min = $10$ min.

questions.question_text: A flight of Jet Airways from Delhi to Mumbai has an average speed of 700 km/hr without any stoppage, whereas a flight of Kingfisher from Delhi to Mumbai has an average speed of 560 km/hr with stoppage at Baroda. What is the average stoppage time per hour of Kingfisher flight if both the planes fly at the same speed?
questions.solution:
Due to stoppage, Kingfisher flight covers $(700 - 560)$
$= 140$ km less per hour.
Time taken to cover $140$ km = $\left(\frac{140}{700}\times60\right)$ min = $12$ min.
Hence, stoppage time per hour = $12$ min.

questions.question_text: A bus covered a certain distance from village A to village B at the speed of 60 km/hr. However on its return journey it got stuck in traffic and covered the same distance at the speed of 40 km/hr and took 2 hours more to reach its destination. What is the distance covered between villages A and B?
questions.solution:
Let the distance between village $A$ and $B$ be $x$ km.
Then, $\frac{x}{40} - \frac{x}{60} = 2 \Rightarrow x = 240$.

questions.question_text: A train covers a distance between two stations A and B in 45 minutes. If the speed of the train is reduced by 5 km/hr, then it covers the distance in 48 minutes. The distance between the stations A and B is
questions.solution:
Time taken = 45 min = $ rac{3}{4}$ hr.

Original speed = $ rac{x}{ rac{3}{4}}$ km/hr = $ rac{4x}{3}$ km/hr.

New speed = $ rac{4x}{3} - 5$ km/hr = $ rac{4x-15}{3}$ km/hr.

$ rac{x}{ rac{4x-15}{3}} =  rac{48}{60} 	imes  rac{4x}{4x-15} =  rac{4}{5}$

$ rac{3x}{4x-15} =  rac{4}{5}$

$15x = 16x - 60 ightarrow x = 60$.

questions.question_text: A train travels a distance of 600 km at a constant speed. If the speed of the train is increased by 5 km/hr, the journey would take 4 hours less. Find the speed of the train.
questions.solution:
Let the speed of the train be $x$ km/hr.

Then, $ rac{600}{x} -  rac{600}{x+5} = 4 ightarrow 4(x+5) = 3000

$4x^2 + 20x - 3000 = 0 ightarrow x^2 + 5x - 750 = 0$

$(x+30)(x-25) = 0 ightarrow x = 25$.

Speed of the train = 25 km/hr.

questions.question_text: A car takes 15 minutes less to cover a distance of 75 km, if it increases its speed by 10 km/hr from its usual speed. How much time would it take to cover a distance of 300 km using this speed?
questions.solution:
Let the usual speed be $x$ km/hr.

Then, $ rac{75}{x} -  rac{75}{x+10} =  rac{15}{60} ightarrow x(x+10) = 3000$

$x^2 + 10x - 3000 = 0 ightarrow (x+60)(x-50) = 0 ightarrow x = 50$.

Required time = $ rac{300}{60}$ hrs = 5 hrs.

questions.question_text: With a uniform speed a car covers the distance in 8 hours. Had the speed been increased by 4 km/hr, the same distance could have been covered in $7\frac{1}{2}$ hours. What is the distance covered?
questions.solution:
Let the distance be $x$ km.

Then, $ rac{x}{7} -  rac{x}{8} = 4 ightarrow  rac{x}{56} = 4 ightarrow x = 480$ km.

questions.question_text: Two men start together to walk to a certain destination, one at 3 kmph and another at 3.75 kmph. The latter arrives half an hour before the former. The distance is
questions.solution:
Let the distance be $x$ km.

Then, $ rac{x}{3} -  rac{x}{3.75} =  rac{1}{2} ightarrow 2.5x - 2x = 3.75$

$x =  rac{3.75}{0.50} =  rac{15}{2} = 7.5$ km.

questions.question_text: If a person walks at 14 km/hr instead of 10 km/hr, he would have walked 20 km more. The actual distance travelled by him is
questions.solution:
Let the actual distance travelled be $x$ km.

Then, $ rac{x}{10} -  rac{x}{14} = 20 ightarrow  rac{4x}{140} = 200 ightarrow 4x = 200 ightarrow x = 50$ km.

questions.question_text: In covering a certain distance, the speeds of A and B are in the ratio of 3 : 4. A takes 30 minutes more than B to reach the destination. The time taken by A to reach the destination is
questions.solution:
Ratio of speeds = 3 : 4. Ratio of times taken = 4 : 3.

Suppose A takes $4x$ hrs and B takes $3x$ hrs to reach the destination. Then,

$4x - 3x =  rac{30}{60} =  rac{1}{2}$ or $x =  rac{1}{2}$.

Time taken by A = $4x$ hrs = $4 	imes  rac{1}{2}$ hrs = 2 hrs.

questions.question_text: In covering a distance of 30 km, Abhay takes 2 hours more than Sameer. If Abhay doubles his speed, then he would take 1 hour less than Sameer. Abhay’s speed is
questions.solution:
Let Abhay's speed be $x$ km/hr.

Then, $ rac{30}{x} -  rac{30}{2x} = 3 ightarrow 6 = 30 ightarrow x = 5$ km/hr.

questions.question_text: Three persons are walking from a place A to another place B. Their speeds are in the ratio of 4 : 3 : 5. The time ratio to reach B by these persons will be
questions.solution:
Ratio of speeds = 4 : 3 : 5.

Ratio of times taken = $ rac{1}{4} :  rac{1}{3} :  rac{1}{5} = 15 : 20 : 12$.

questions.question_text: A journey of 192 km between two cities takes 2 hours less by a fast train than by a slow train. If the average speed of the slow train is 16 km/hr less than that of the fast train, then the average speed of the fast train is
questions.solution:
Let the speed of the fast train be $x$ km/hr.

Then, speed of the slow train = $(x - 16)$ km/hr.

$ rac{192}{x-16} -  rac{192}{x} = 2 ightarrow  rac{1}{x-16} -  rac{1}{x} =  rac{1}{96}$

$x^2 - 16x - 1536 = 0 ightarrow (x - 48)(x + 32) = 0 ightarrow x = 48$.

questions.question_text: In a flight of 6000 km, an aircraft was slowed down due to bad weather. The average speed for the trip was reduced by 400 kmph and the time of flight increased by 30 minutes. The original planned duration of the flight was
questions.solution:
Let the original planned duration of the flight be $x$ hours.

Then, $ rac{6000}{x} -  rac{6000}{x +  rac{1}{2}} = 400 ightarrow  rac{15}{x} -  rac{30}{2x+1} = 1$

$2x^2 + x - 15 = 0$

$(x + 3)(2x - 5) = 0 ightarrow x =  rac{5}{2} = 2.5$.

questions.question_text: An aeroplane first flew with a speed of 440 kmph and covered a certain distance. It still had to cover 770 km less than what it had already covered, but it flew with a speed of 660 kmph. The average speed for the entire flight was 500 kmph. Find the total distance covered.
questions.solution:
Let the distance covered at 440 kmph be $x$ km.

Then, distance covered at 660 kmph = $(x - 770)$ km.

Total distance covered = $(x + x - 770)$ km = $(2x - 770)$ km.

$ rac{2x - 770}{500} =  rac{x}{440} +  rac{x - 770}{660} ightarrow  rac{2x - 770}{25} =  rac{x}{22} +  rac{x - 770}{33}$

$66(2x - 770) = 25(5x - 1540) ightarrow 7x = 12320 ightarrow x = 1760$.

Hence, total distance covered = $(2 	imes 1760 - 770)$ km = 2750 km.

questions.question_text: Two boys A and B start at the same time to ride from Delhi to Meerut, 60 km away. A travels 4 km an hour slower than B, B reaches Meerut and at once turns back meeting A, 12 km from Meerut. A’s rate was
questions.solution:
Let A's speed = $x$ km/hr.

Then, B's speed = $(x + 4)$ km/hr.

Clearly, time taken by B to cover $(60 + 12)$ i.e., 72 km

= Time taken by A to cover $(60 - 12)$ i.e., 48 km

$ rac{72}{x+4} =  rac{48}{x} ightarrow 72x = 48x + 192 ightarrow 24x = 192 ightarrow x = 8$.

Hence, A's speed = 8 km/hr.

questions.question_text: A cyclist drove one kilometre, with the wind in his back, in 3 minutes and drove the same way back, against the wind, in 4 minutes. If we assume that the cyclist always puts constant force on the pedals, how much time would it take him to drive 1 km without wind?
questions.solution:
Then, $ rac{1}{x+y} =  rac{3}{60} ightarrow x+y = 20$ ...(i)

And, $ rac{1}{x-y} =  rac{4}{60} ightarrow x-y = 15$ ...(ii)

Adding (i) and (ii), we get: $2x = 35$ or $x = 17.5$.

Putting $x = 17.5$ in (i), we get: $y = 2.5$.

Time taken to drive 17.5 km without wind = 1 hr.

Time taken to drive 1 km without wind

= $ rac{1}{17.5} 	imes 60$ min = $3 rac{3}{7}$ min.

questions.question_text: Ramesh travels 760 km to his home, partly by train and partly by car. He takes 8 hours, if he travels 160 km by train and the rest by car. He takes 12 minutes more, if he travels 240 km by train and the rest by car. What are the speeds of the car and the train respectively?
questions.solution:
Let the speeds of the train and the car be $x$ km/hr and $y$ km/hr respectively.

Then, $ rac{160}{x} +  rac{600}{y} = 8 ightarrow  rac{20}{x} +  rac{75}{y} = 1$ ...(i)

And, $ rac{240}{x} +  rac{520}{y} = 11 ightarrow  rac{240}{x} +  rac{520}{y} = 41$ ...(ii)

Multiplying (i) by 12 and subtracting (ii) from it, we get:

$ rac{380}{y} = 12 - 41 = 19 ightarrow y =  rac{380 	imes 5}{19} = 100$.

Putting $y = 100$ in (i), we get: $ rac{20}{x} +  rac{3}{4} = 1 ightarrow  rac{20}{x} =  rac{1}{4} ightarrow x = 80$.

Hence, speed of car = 100 km/hr, speed of train = 80 km/hr.

questions.question_text: Two sea trawlers left a sea port simultaneously in two mutually perpendicular directions. Half an hour later, the shortest distance between them was 17 km, and another 15 minutes later, one sea trawler was 10.5 km farther from the origin than the other. Find the speed of each sea trawler.
questions.solution:
Suppose the two trawlers start from a point O and move in the directions OA and OB respectively.

Let the speeds of the two sea trawlers be $x$ km/hr and $y$ km/hr respectively.

Then, $\left(x 	imes  rac{1}{2}\right)^2 + \left(y 	imes  rac{1}{2}\right)^2 = (17)^2$

$ rac{x^2}{4} +  rac{y^2}{4} = 289 ightarrow x^2 + y^2 = 1156$ ...(i)

And, $\left(x 	imes  rac{3}{4}\right) - \left(y 	imes  rac{3}{4}\right) = 10.5 ightarrow x - y = 10.5 	imes  rac{4}{3} = 14$ ...(ii)

Now, $(x + y)^2 + (x - y)^2 = 2(x^2 + y^2)$

$(x + y)^2 = 2 	imes 1156 - (14)^2 = 2312 - 196 = 2116$

$x + y = \sqrt{2116} = 46$ ...(iii)

Adding (ii) and (iii), we get: $2x = 60$ or $x = 30$.

Putting $x = 30$ in (ii), we get: $y = 16$.

Hence, the speeds of the two sea-trawlers are 30 km/hr and 16 km/hr.

questions.question_text: A runs twice as fast as B and B runs thrice as fast as C. The distance covered by C in 72 minutes, will be covered by A in
questions.solution:
Let C's speed = $x$ km/hr. Then, B's speed = $3x$ km/hr and A's speed = $6x$ km/hr.

Ratio of speeds of A, B, C = $6x : 3x : x = 6 : 3 : 1$.

Ratio of times taken = $ rac{1}{6} :  rac{1}{3} : 1 = 1 : 2 : 6$.

If C takes 6 min, then A takes 1 min.

If C takes 72 min, then A takes $ rac{1}{6} 	imes 72$ min = 12 min.

questions.question_text: A ship, 40 kilometres from the shore, springs a leak which admits $3\frac{3}{4}$ tonnes of water in 12 minutes. 60 tonnes would suffice to sink her, but the ship’s pumps can throw out 12 tonnes of water in one hour. Find the average rate of sailing, so that she may reach the shore just as she begins to sink.
questions.solution:
Quantity of water let in by the leak in 1 min

= $\left(\frac{3}{12} - \frac{1}{4}\right)$ tonnes = $\frac{15}{48}$ tonnes.

Quantity of water thrown out by the pumps in 1 min

= $\frac{12}{60}$ tonnes = $\frac{1}{5}$ tonnes.

Net quantity of water filled in the ship in 1 min

= $\left(\frac{15}{48} - \frac{1}{5}\right)$ tonnes = $\frac{27}{240}$ tonnes.

$\frac{27}{240}$ tonnes water is filled in 1 min.

60 tonnes water is filled in $\frac{240}{27} \times 60$ min

= $\frac{1600}{3}$ min = $\frac{80}{9}$ hrs.

Hence, required speed = 40 km/hr.

questions.question_text: Amit travelled back to home in a car, after visiting his friend in a distant village. When he started at his friend’s house the car had exactly 18 litres of petrol in it. He travelled along at a steady 40 kilometres per hour and managed a 10 kilometres per litre of petrol. As the car was old, the fuel tank lost fuel at the rate of half a litre per hour. Amit was lucky as his car stopped just in front of his home because it had run out of fuel and he only just made it. How far was it from his friend’s home to Amit’s home?
questions.solution:
Quantity of petrol consumed in 1 hour

= $\left(\frac{40}{10} + \frac{1}{2}\right)$ litres = $4\frac{1}{2}$ litres.

Time for which the fuel lasted = $\frac{18}{4\frac{1}{2}}$ hrs

= $18 \times \frac{2}{9}$ hrs = 4 hrs.

Required distance = $(40 \times 4)$ km = 160 km.

questions.question_text: Two men starting from the same place walk at the rate of 5 kmph and 5.5 kmph respectively. What time will they take to be 8.5 km apart, if they walk in the same direction?
questions.solution:
To be 0.5 km apart, they take 1 hour.

To be 8.5 km apart, they take $\frac{1}{0.5} \times 8.5$ hrs = 17 hrs.

questions.question_text: A walks around a circular field at the rate of one round per hour while B runs around it at the rate of six rounds per hour. They start in the same direction from the same point at 7.30 a.m. They shall first cross each other at :
questions.solution:
Since A and B move in the same direction along the circle,
so they will first meet each other when there is a difference of one round between the two.
Relative speed of A and B = $(6-1)=5$ rounds per hour.
Time taken to complete one round at this speed
$=  rac{1}{5}$ hr $= 12$ min.

questions.question_text: A and B start from the same point and in the same direction at 7 a.m. to walk around a rectangular field 400 m $\times$ 300 m. A and B walk at the rate of 3 km/hr and 2.5 km/hr respectively. How many times shall they cross each other if they continue to walk till 12 : 30 p.m.?
questions.solution:
Perimeter of the field $= 2(400+300)$ m $= 1400$ m $= 1.4$ km.
Since A and B move in the same direction, so they will
first meet each other when there is a difference of one
round i.e., $1.4$ km between the two.
Relative speed of A and B $= (3-2.5)$ km/hr $= 0.5$ km/hr.
Time take to cover $1.4$ km at this speed $=  rac{1.4}{0.5}$ hr
$= 2 rac{4}{5}$ hr $= 2$ hr $48$ min.
So they shall first cross each other at $9:48$ a.m. and again,
$2$ hr $48$ min after $9:48$ a.m. i.e., $12:36$ p.m.
Thus, till $12:30$ p.m. they will cross each other once.

questions.passage_text: There are 8 equidistant points A, B, C, D, E, F, G and H in the clockwise direction on the periphery of a circle. In a time interval t, a person reaches from A to C with uniform motion while another person reaches the point E from the point B during the same time interval with uniform motion. Both the persons move in the same direction along the circumference of the circle and start at the same instant.
questions.question_text: How much time after the start, will the two persons meet each other?
questions.solution:
Distance covered by first person in time $t$
$=  rac{2}{8}$ rounds $=  rac{1}{4}$ round.

questions.question_text: A walks at 4 kmph and 4 hours after his start, B cycles after him at 10 kmph. How far from the start does B catch up with A?
questions.solution:
Suppose after $x$ km from the start B catches up with A.
Then, the difference in the time taken by A to cover $x$ km
and that taken by B to cover $x$ km is $4$ hours.
$\therefore  rac{x}{4} -  rac{x}{10} = 4$ or $x = 26.7$ km.
