questions.question_text: Paschim Express left Delhi for Mumbai at 14.30 hrs travelling at a speed of 60 kmph and August Kranti Express left Delhi for Mumbai on the same day at 16.30 hrs travelling at a speed of 80 kmph. How far away from Delhi will the two trains meet (excluding stoppages)?
questions.solution:
Suppose the two trains meet $x$ km from Delhi.
Then, $ rac{x}{60} -  rac{x}{80} = 2 \Leftrightarrow x = 480$.

questions.question_text: A thief is noticed by a policeman from a distance of 200 m. The thief starts running and the policeman chases him. The thief and the policeman run at the rate of 10 km and 11 km per hour respectively. What is the distance between them after 6 minutes?
questions.solution:
Relative speed of the thief and policeman
$= (11-10)$ km/hr $= 1$ km/hr.
Distance covered in $6$ minutes $=  rac{1}{60} \times 6$ km
$=  rac{1}{10}$ km $= 100$ m.
$\therefore$ Distance between the thief and policeman
$= (200-100)$ m $= 100$ m.

questions.question_text: A bus is moving with a speed of 30 km/hr ahead of a car with a speed of 50 km/hr. How many kilometres apart are they if it takes 15 minutes for the car to catch up with the bus?
questions.solution:
Relative speed of the car w.r.t. bus $= (50-30)$ km/hr
$= 20$ km/hr.
Required distance = Distance covered in $15$ min at relative
speed $= 20 \times  rac{1}{4}$ km $= 5$ km.

questions.question_text: A thief running at 8 km/hr is chased by a policeman whose speed is 10 km/hr. If the thief is 100 metres ahead of the policeman, then the time required for the policeman to catch the thief will be
questions.solution:
Relative speed $= (10-8)$ km/hr $= 2$ km/hr.
Required time = Time taken to cover $100$ m at relative
speed
$=  rac{100}{2000}$ hr $=  rac{1}{20}$ hr $= 60 \times  rac{1}{20}$ min $= 3$ min.

questions.question_text: A thief steals a car at 2.30 p.m. and drives it at 60 kmph. The theft is discovered at 3 p.m. and the owner sets off in another car at 75 kmph. When will he overtake the thief?
questions.solution:
Suppose the thief is overtaken $x$ hrs after $2.30$ p.m.
Then, Distance covered by the thief in $x$ hrs = Distance
covered by the owner in $\left(x-\frac{1}{2}\right)$ hrs.
$\therefore 60x = 75 \left(x-\frac{1}{2}\right) \Leftrightarrow 15x = \frac{75}{2} \Leftrightarrow x =  rac{5}{2}$ hrs.
So, the thief is overtaken at $5$ p.m.

questions.question_text: Aryan runs at a speed of 40 metres/minute. Rahul follows him after an interval of 5 minutes and runs at a speed of 50 metres/minute. Rahul’s dog runs at a speed of 60 metres/minute and starts along with Rahul. The dog reaches Aryan and then comes back to Rahul, and continues to do so till Rahul reaches Aryan. What is the total distance covered by the dog?
questions.solution:
Distance covered by Aryan in $5$ min $= (40 \times 5)$ m $= 200$ m.
Relative speed of Rahul w.r.t. Aryan $= (50-40)$ m/min
$= 10$ m/min.
Time taken to cover $200$ m at relative speed
$=  rac{200}{10}$ min $= 20$ min.
Distance covered by the dog in $20$ min $= (60 \times 20)$ m
$= 1200$ m.

questions.question_text: A thief, pursued by a policeman, was 100 m ahead at the start. If the ratio of the speed of the policeman to that of the thief was 5 : 4, then how far could the thief go before he was caught by the policeman?
questions.solution:
Let the thief be caught $x$ metres from the place where the
policeman started running.
Let the speed of the policeman and the thief be $5y$ m/s
and $4y$ m/s respectively.
Then, time taken by the policeman to cover $x$ metres
= time taken by the thief to cover $(x-100)$ m
$\Rightarrow  rac{x}{5y} =  rac{x-100}{4y} \Rightarrow 4x = 5(x-100) \Rightarrow x = 500$.
So, the thief ran $(500-100)$ i.e. $400$ m before being caught.

questions.question_text: A walks at a uniform rate of 4 km an hour; and 4 hours after his start, B bicycles after him at the uniform rate of 10 km an hour. How far from the starting point will B catch A?
questions.solution:
Distance covered by A in $4$ hrs $= (4 \times 4)$ km $= 16$ km.
Relative speed of B w.r.t. A $= (10-4)$ km/hr $= 6$ km/hr.
Time taken to cover $16$ km at relative speed
$=  rac{16}{6}$ hrs $=  rac{8}{3}$ hrs.
Distance covered by B in $\frac{8}{3}$ hrs $= \left(10 \times \frac{8}{3}\right)$ km $= \frac{80}{3}$ km
$= 26.7$ km.

questions.question_text: A passenger train runs at the rate of 80 kmph. It starts from the station, 6 hours after a goods train leaves the station. The passenger train overtakes the goods train after 4 hours. The speed of goods train is
questions.solution:
Let the speed of the goods train be $x$ km/hr. Then, rela-
tive speed $= (80-x)$ km/hr.
Distance covered by goods train in $6$ hrs at $x$ km/hr
= Distance covered by passenger train in $4$ hrs at $(80-x)$
km/hr
$\Rightarrow 6x = 4(80-x) \Rightarrow 10x = 320 \Rightarrow x = 32$ km/hr.

questions.passage_text: An athlete claimed that his timing for a 100 m dash should be corrected because the starting signal was given by a gun fired from a point 10 m away from him and the timekeeper was standing close to the gun. The error due to this could be (in seconds) [Given: speed of sound = 300 m/s]
questions.question_text: An athlete claimed that his timing for a 100 m dash should be corrected because the starting signal was given by a gun fired from a point 10 m away from him and the timekeeper was standing close to the gun. The error due to this could be (in seconds) [Given: speed of sound = 300 m/s]
questions.solution:
Error = Time taken to cover $10$ m at $300$ m/sec
$=  rac{10}{300}$ sec $=  rac{1}{30}$ sec $\approx 0.03$ sec.

questions.question_text: Sound waves travel at 300 m/s. Sound produced at a point is heard by a person after 5 seconds while the same sound is heard by another person after 6 seconds. What could be the maximum and minimum distance between the two persons?
questions.solution:
Clearly, the two persons would be maximum distance
apart when they stand in opposite directions to the point
at which sound is produced, and minimum distance apart
when they stand in the same direction.
$\therefore$ Maximum distance between the two persons
= Distance covered by sound in $(6+5)$ seconds, i.e. $11$ sec
$= (300 \times 11)$ m $= 3300$ m $= 3.3$ km.
And, minimum distance between the two persons
= Distance covered by sound in $(6-5)$ sec., i.e. $1$ sec
$= 300$ m $= 0.3$ km.

questions.question_text: Two guns were fired from the same place at an interval of 8 minutes, A person approaching the place observes that 5 minutes 52 seconds have elapsed between the hearing of the sound of the two guns. If the velocity of the sound is 330 m/sec, the man was approaching the place at what speed (in km/hr)?
questions.solution:
Let the speed of the man be $x$ m/sec.
Then, Distance travelled by the man in $5$ min $52$ sec
= Distance travelled by sound in $8$ sec
$\Leftrightarrow x \times 352 = 330 \times 8$
$\Leftrightarrow x = \left(\frac{330 \times 8}{352}\right)$ m/sec.
$= \left(\frac{330 \times 8}{352} \times \frac{18}{5}\right)$ km/hr $= 27$ km/hr.

questions.question_text: Two cyclists start from the same place in opposite directions. One goes towards north at 18 kmph and the other goes towards south at 20 kmph. What time will they take to be 47.5 km apart?
questions.solution:
To be $(18+20)$ km apart, they take $1$ hour.
To be $47.5$ km apart, they take $\frac{47.5}{38}$ hrs $= 1\frac{1}{4}$ hrs.

questions.question_text: A and B are two stations 10 km apart. A man, P starts from A and travels towards B at the rate of 3 km/hr, whereas another man Q starts from B and travels to wards A at the rate of 2 km/hr. When and where do they meet?
questions.solution:
Suppose they meet after $x$ hours. Then,
Distance travelled by P in $x$ hrs + Distance travelled by
Q in $x$ hrs $= 10$ km
$\Leftrightarrow 3x + 2x = 10 \Rightarrow 5x = 10 \Rightarrow x = 2$ hrs.
Distance travelled by P in $2$ hrs $= (3 \times 2)$ km $= 6$ km.

questions.question_text: Two cars X and Y start from places A and B respectively which are 700 km apart at 9 a.m. Both the cars run at an average speed of 60 km/hr. Car X stops at 10 a.m. and again starts at 11 a.m. while the other car continues to run without stopping. The two cars cross each other at
questions.solution:
Suppose they meet $x$ hrs after $9$ a.m. Then,
Distance travelled by car X in $(x-1)$ hrs + Distance trav-
elled by car Y in $x$ hrs $= 700$ km
$\Rightarrow 60(x-1) + 60x = 700 \Rightarrow 120x = 760$
$\Rightarrow x = \frac{760}{120} = \frac{19}{3}$ hrs $= 6$ hr $20$ min.
So, they cross each other $6$ hr $20$ min after $9$ a.m. i.e., at
$3:20$ p.m.

questions.question_text: A train started from station A and proceeded towards station B at a speed of 48 km/hr. Forty five minutes later another train started from station B and proceeded towards station a at 50 km/hr. If the distance between the two stations is 232 km, at what distance from station A will the trains meet?
questions.solution:
Suppose the trains meet after $x$ hrs. Then,
Distance covered by 1st train in $x$ hrs
+ Distance covered by 2nd train in $\left(x-\frac{3}{4}\right)$ hrs $= 232$ km
$\Rightarrow 48x + 50 \left(x-\frac{3}{4}\right) = 232$
$\Rightarrow 98x = 232 + \frac{75}{2} = \frac{539}{2} \Rightarrow x = \frac{539}{196}$ hrs.
Required distance
= Distance travelled by 1st train in $\frac{539}{196}$ hrs.
$= \left(48 \times \frac{539}{196}\right)$ km $= 132$ km.

questions.question_text: The jogging track in a sports complex is 726 metres in circumference. Deepak and his wife start from the same point and walk in opposite directions at 4.5 km/hr and 3.75 km/hr respectively. They will meet for the first time in
questions.solution:
Clearly, the two will meet when they are 726 m apart.
To be (4.5 + 3.75) = 8.25 km apart, they take 1 hour.
To be 726 m apart, they take $\left(\frac{100}{825}\times\frac{726}{1000}\right)$ hrs
$= \left(\frac{242}{2750}\times60\right)$ min = 5.28 min.

questions.question_text: A and B walk around a circular track. They start at 8 a.m. from the same point in the opposite directions. A and B walk at a speed of 2 rounds per hour and 3 rounds per hour respectively. How many times shall they cross each other before 9.30 a.m.?
questions.solution:
Relative speed = (2 + 3) = 5 rounds per hour.
So, they cross each other 5 times in an hour and 2 times in half an hour.
Hence, they cross each other 7 times before 9.30 a.m.

questions.question_text: Two cyclists start on a circular track from a given point but in opposite directions with speeds of 7 m/sec and 8 m/sec respectively. If the circumference of the circle is 300 metres, after what time will they meet at the starting point?
questions.solution:
Time taken by the two cyclists to cover one round of the track is $\frac{300}{7}$ sec and $\frac{300}{8}$ sec respectively.
$\therefore$ Required time = L.C.M. of $\frac{300}{7}$ and $\frac{300}{8}$ = 300 sec.

questions.question_text: A distance of 425 km separates two trains moving towards each other at a speed of 200 km/hr each. What will be the distance between them after 1 hr 30 min, if they reduce their speed by half, every half an hour?
questions.solution:
Relative speed = (200 + 200) km/hr = 400 km/hr.
Distance covered in 1 hr 30 min
$= \left(400\times\frac{1}{2} + 200\times\frac{1}{2} + 100\times\frac{1}{2}\right)$ km
$= (200 + 100 + 50)$ km = 350 km.
[$\because$ speed reduces by half every half an hour]
Hence, distance between the trains
$= (425 - 350)$ km = 75 km.

questions.passage_text:
Directions (Questions 145 to 147): These questions are based on the following information:
P and Q are 120 km apart. A starts from P towards Q at 6 a.m. B starts from Q towards P at 11 a.m. on the same day. A is 50 % faster than B. They cross each other at 8 p.m.
questions.question_text: At what time will A reach his destination?
questions.solution:
Time taken by A to cover 120 km = $\frac{120}{6}$ hrs = 20 hrs.
So, A will reach his destination 20 hrs after 6 a.m., i.e., at 2 a.m. the next day.

questions.passage_text:
Directions (Questions 145 to 147): These questions are based on the following information:
P and Q are 120 km apart. A starts from P towards Q at 6 a.m. B starts from Q towards P at 11 a.m. on the same day. A is 50 % faster than B. They cross each other at 8 p.m.
questions.question_text: In reaching his destination, how many more hours than A, will B take?
questions.solution:
Required difference = $\left(\frac{120}{4} - \frac{120}{6}\right)$ hrs
$= (30 - 20)$ hrs = 10 hrs.

questions.passage_text:
Directions (Questions 145 to 147): These questions are based on the following information:
P and Q are 120 km apart. A starts from P towards Q at 6 a.m. B starts from Q towards P at 11 a.m. on the same day. A is 50 % faster than B. They cross each other at 8 p.m.
questions.question_text: A’s speed (in km/hr) is
questions.solution: A's speed = 6 km/hr.

questions.question_text: Train X leaves New York at 1 A.M. and travels east at a constant speed of x mph. If train Z leaves New York at 2 A.M. and travels east, at what constant rate of speed will train Z have to travel in order to catch train X at exactly 5 : 30 A.M.?
questions.solution:
Let the speed of train Z be z mph.
Distance travelled by train X in 1 hr = x miles.
Relative speed of train Z w.r.t. train X = (z - x) mph.
To catch train X at 5 : 30 A.M., train Z will have to cover x miles at relative speed in 3 hr 30 min, i.e. $\frac{7}{2}$ hrs.
$\therefore (z - x) \times \frac{7}{2} = x \Rightarrow z - x = \frac{2x}{7} \Rightarrow z = \frac{9x}{7}$

questions.question_text: Two ladies simultaneously leave cities A and B connected by a straight road and travel towards each other. The first lady travels 2 km/hr faster than the second lady and reaches B one hour before the second lady reaches A. The two cities A and B are 24 km apart. How many kilometres does each lady travel in one hour?
questions.solution:
Let the speed of the second lady be x km/hr.
Then, speed of first lady = (x + 2) km/hr.
$\therefore \frac{24}{x} - \frac{24}{x + 2} = 1 \Rightarrow x(x + 2) = 48$
$\Rightarrow x^2 + 2x - 48 = 0 \Rightarrow x^2 + 8x - 6x - 48 = 0$
$\Rightarrow x(x + 8) - 6(x + 8) = 0 \Rightarrow (x + 8)(x - 6) = 0$
$\Rightarrow x = 6$.
Hence, speed of first lady = 8 km/hr; speed of second lady = 6 km/hr.

questions.question_text: Buses start from a bus terminal with a speed of 20 km/hr at intervals of 10 minutes. What is the speed of a man coming from the opposite direction towards the bus terminal if he meets the buses at intervals of 8 minutes?
questions.solution:
Let the speed of the man be x km/hr.
Then, Distance covered by the bus in 2 min
= Distance covered by the man in 8 min
$\Rightarrow 20 \times \frac{2}{60} = x \times \frac{8}{60} \Rightarrow x = \frac{2 \times 60}{3 \times 8} = 5$ km/hr.

questions.question_text: Two men at points R and S, 76 km apart, set out at the same time to walk towards each other. The man at R walks uniformly at the rate of $1\frac{1}{2}$ km/hr; the man at S walks at the constant rate of $1\frac{3}{4}$ km/hr for the first hour, at $3\frac{3}{4}$ km/hr for the second hour, and so on, in arithmetic progression. If the men meet x km nearer R than S in an integral number of hours, then x is
questions.solution:
Suppose the two men meet in n hours.
Then Sum of distances covered by two men in n hours = 76
$\Rightarrow \left(4\frac{1}{2} + 3\frac{1}{4} + 3\frac{1}{4} + \dots \dots \text{upto n terms}\right) = 76$
$\Rightarrow \frac{n}{2} \left[2 \times \frac{13}{4} + (n - 1) \times \frac{1}{2}\right] = 76$
[Sum to n terms of an A.P.]
$\Rightarrow \frac{n}{2} \left[\frac{13}{2} + \frac{n}{2} - \frac{1}{2}\right] = 76$
$\Rightarrow \frac{n}{2} \left[\frac{12}{2} + \frac{n}{2}\right] = 76$
$\Rightarrow \frac{9n}{2} + \frac{6n}{2} + \frac{n^2}{4} = 76 \Rightarrow \frac{15n}{2} + \frac{n^2}{4} = 76$
$\Rightarrow 30n + n^2 = 304 \Rightarrow n^2 + 30n - 304 = 0$
$\Rightarrow (n + 38)(n - 8) = 0 \Rightarrow n = 8$.
Distance travelled by first man in 8 hours
$= \left(\frac{9}{2} \times 8\right)$ km = 36 km.
Distance travelled by second man = (76 - 36) km = 40 km.
Thus, the meeting point is 36 km from R and 40 km from S, i.e. (40 - 36) = 4 km nearer to R than S.
Hence, x = 4.

questions.question_text: Two planes move along a circle of circumference 1.2 kms with constant speeds. When they move in different directions, they meet every 15 seconds and when they move in the same direction one plane overtakes the other every 60 seconds. The speed of the slower plane is
questions.solution:
Let their speeds be x m/sec and y m/sec respectively.
Then, $\frac{1200}{x + y} = 15 \Rightarrow x + y = 80$
And, $\frac{1200}{x - y} = 60 \Rightarrow x - y = 20$
Adding (i) and (ii), we get : 2x = 100 or x = 50.
Putting x = 50 in (i), we get : y = 30.
Hence, speed of slower plane = 30m/s = 0.03 km/s.

questions.question_text: Two cyclists, k kilometres apart, and starting at the same time, would be together in r hours if they travelled in the same direction, but would pass each other in t hours if they travelled in opposite directions. The ratio of the speed of the faster cyclist to that of the slower is
questions.solution:
Let the speed of the faster and slower cyclists be x km/hr and y km/hr respectively,
Then, $\frac{k}{x - y} = r \Rightarrow x - y = \frac{k}{r}$
And, $\frac{k}{x + y} = t \Rightarrow x + y = \frac{k}{t}$
From (i) and (ii), we have :
(x - y)r = (x + y)t $\Rightarrow$ xr - yr = xt + yt $\Rightarrow$ xr - xt = yr + yt
$\Rightarrow x(r - t) = y(r + t) \Rightarrow \frac{x}{y} = \frac{r + t}{r - t}$

questions.question_text: A bus left X for point Y. Two hours later a car left point X for Y and arrived at Y at the same time as the bus. If the car and the bus left simultaneously from the opposite ends X and Y towards each other, they would meet $1\frac{1}{3}$ hours after the start. How much time did it take the bus to travel from X to Y?
questions.solution:
Let the distance between points X and Y be d km.
Suppose the bus takes x hours to travel from X to Y.
Then, speed of bus = $\frac{d}{x}$ and speed of car = $\frac{d}{x - 2}$.
Now, sum of distances travelled by car and bus in $1\frac{1}{3}$ hrs i.e. $\frac{4}{3}$ hrs = d
$\Rightarrow \left(\frac{d}{x} \times \frac{4}{3} + \frac{d}{x - 2} \times \frac{4}{3}\right) = d$
$\Rightarrow \frac{4}{3x} + \frac{4}{3(x - 2)} = 1$
$\Rightarrow 4(x - 2) + 4x = 3x(x - 2)$
$\Rightarrow 3x^2 - 14x + 8 = 0$
$\Rightarrow 3x^2 - 12x - 2x + 8 = 0 \Rightarrow 3x(x - 4) - 2(x - 4) = 0$
$\Rightarrow (x - 4)(3x - 2) = 0 \Rightarrow x = 4$ [$\because x \neq \frac{2}{3}$]
$\therefore$ Required time = 4 hours.

questions.question_text: Two trains starting at the same time from two stations 200 km apart and going in opposite directions cross each other at a distance of 110 km from one of the stations. What is the ratio of their speeds?
questions.solution:
In the same time, they cover 110 km and 90 km respectively.
$\therefore$ Ratio of their speeds = 110 : 90 = 11 : 9.

questions.question_text: Two trains start from stations A and B and travel towards each other at a speed of 50 kmph and 60 kmph respectively. A the time of their meeting, the second train had travelled 120 km more than the first. The distance between A and B is
questions.solution:
At the time of meeting, let the distance travelled by the first train be x km.
Then, distance covered by the second train = (x + 120) km.
$\therefore \frac{x}{50} = \frac{x + 120}{60} \Rightarrow 60x = 50x + 6000$
$\Rightarrow 10x = 6000 \Rightarrow x = 600$.
So, distance between A and B = (x + x + 120) km = 1320 km.

questions.question_text: Train A leaves Ludhiana for Delhi at 11 a.m, running at the speed of 60 km/hr. Train B leaves Ludhiana for Delhi by the same route at 2 p.m. on the same day, running at the speed of 72 km/hr. At what time will the two trains meet each other?
questions.solution:
Distance covered by train A from 11 a.m. to 2 p.m. i.e., in 3 hrs = (60 $\times$ 3) = 180 km.
Relative speed = (72 - 60) km/hr = 12 km/hr.
Time taken to cover 180 km at relative speed
$= \frac{180}{12}$ hrs = 15 hrs.
So, the two trains will meet 15 hrs after 2 p.m. i.e., at 5 a.m. on the next day.

questions.question_text: A train M leaves station X at 5 a.m and reaches station Y at 9 a.m. Another train N leaves station Y at 7 a.m. and reaches station X at 10.30 a.m. At what time do the two trains cross each other?
questions.solution:
Let the distance between stations X and Y be x km and let the trains meet y hours after 7 a.m.
Clearly, M covers x km in 4 hrs and N covers x km in (7/2) hrs.
$\therefore$ Speed of M = $\frac{x}{4}$ kmph, Speed of N = $\frac{2x}{7}$ kmph.
Distance covered by M in (y + 2) hrs + Distance covered by N in y hrs = x.
$\therefore \frac{x}{4}(y + 2) + \frac{2x}{7}y = x \Rightarrow \frac{y + 2}{4} + \frac{2y}{7} = 1$
$\Rightarrow y = \frac{14}{15}$ hrs = 56 min.
Hence, the trains meet at 7.56 a.m.

questions.question_text: Train A travelling at 60 km/hr leaves Mumbai for Delhi at 6 p.m. Train B travelling at 90 km/hr also leaves Mumbai for Delhi at 9 p.m. Train C leaves Delhi for Mumbai at 9 p.m. If all the three trains meet at the same time between Mumbai and Delhi, then what is the speed of train C if the distance between Delhi and Mumbai is 1260 km?
questions.solution:
Suppose the three trains meet x hours after 9 p.m.
Let the speed of train C be y km/hr.
Distance travelled by Train A in (x + 3) hrs = Distance travelled by Train B in x hrs
$\Rightarrow 60(x + 3) = 90x \Rightarrow 30x = 180 \Rightarrow x = 6$.
Also, dist. travelled by Train B in x hrs + Dist. travelled by train C in x hrs = 1260 km
$\Rightarrow 90x + yx = 1260 \Rightarrow 540 + 6y = 1260 \Rightarrow 6y = 720 \Rightarrow y = 120$.
Hence, speed of Train C = 120 km/hr.

questions.question_text: Amit starts from a point A and walks to another point B and then returns from B to A by his car and thus takes a total time of 6 hours and 45 minutes. If he had driven both ways in his car, he would have taken 2 hours less. How long would it take for him to walk both ways?
questions.solution:
Let the distance be x km. Then,
(Time taken to walk x km) + (Time taken to drive x km) = $\frac{27}{4}$ hrs
$\Rightarrow$ (Time taken to walk 2x km) + (Time taken to drive 2x km) = $\frac{27}{2}$ hrs.
But time taken to drive 2x km = $\frac{19}{4}$ hrs.
$\therefore$ Time taken to walk 2x km = $\left(\frac{27}{2} - \frac{19}{4}\right)$ hrs
$= \frac{35}{4}$ hrs = 8 hrs 45 min.

questions.question_text: Reena leaves office at 6.00 p.m. and catches a 6.30 p.m. local train that arrives in her town at 7.00 p.m. Her father leaves home to pick her up at 7.00 p.m. from the station as she gets off the train. Yesterday, Reena left her office early and took a 6.00 p.m. train and arrived at 6.30 p.m. As her father was not there to pick her up, she started walking towards home. Her father left home at the usual time, saw her daughter walking, turned around, picked her up and drove home, arriving there 10 minutes earlier than the usual. For how long did Reena walk before her father picked her up?
questions.solution: Since 10 minutes are saved, it means that Reena’s father drives from the meeting point to the station and back to the meeting point in 10 min i.e. he can drive from the meeting point to the station in $ rac{10}{2} = 5$ min.

questions.question_text: On return from a business trip, Mr X was to be picked up from the railway station by his coachman. However, his meeting having finished before schedule, he left his destination earlier by catching an earlier train as a result of which he arrived 2 hours early. Immediately on arrival he rang up home for the coach and was told that it had just left in order to be exactly in time for the train by which he was scheduled to come. To save time he started walking home ward a 4 mph. On the way he met the coachman who brought him home an hour before schedule. How far is Mr X’s house from the railway station?
questions.solution:
Clearly, the coachman needed 2 hours to reach the station from Mr X’s house or 4 hours for the entire round trip.
But now he took $(4 - 1) = 3$ hours for the round trip.
Thus he went one way in $1 rac{1}{2}$ hrs i.e.,
Mr X walked for $1 rac{1}{2}$ hrs.
Distance covered in $1 rac{1}{2}$ hrs while walking
$=  rac{3}{2} 	imes 4$ miles $= 6$ miles.
But this distance would have been covered by the coachman in $(2 - 1 rac{1}{2}) =  rac{1}{2}$ hr.
Speed of the coachman $= 6 	imes 2$ mph $= 12$ mph.
$\therefore$ Distance between the house and the station
$= $ Distance covered by coachman in 2 hrs
$= (12 	imes 2)$ miles $= 24$ miles.

questions.question_text: A train approaches a tunnel AB. Inside the tunnel is a cat located at a point that is $\frac{3}{8}$ of the distance AB measured from the entrance A. When the train whistles, that cat runs. If the cat moves to the entrance A of the tunnel, the train catches the cat exactly at the entrance. If the cat moves to the exit B, the train catches the cat at exactly the exit. The ratio of the speed of the train to that of the cat is of the order
questions.solution:
Let length $AB = x$.
Then, if $C$ is the position of the cat, we have $AC =  rac{3}{8}x$.
When the cat runs towards the entrance, the train catches it at the entrance. This means that when the train reaches the entrance, the cat has travelled a distance of $ rac{3}{8}x$.
Let us now consider the case when the cat runs towards the exit.
So, when the train reaches $A$, the cat reaches a point $D$ such that $CD =  rac{3}{8}x$.
Then, $BD =  rac{x - ( rac{3}{8}x +  rac{3}{8}x) =  rac{x}{4}$.
Since the train catches the cat at the exit, so the train covers distance $x$ ($= AB$) in the same time in which the cat covers distance $ rac{x}{4}$ ($= BD$).
$\therefore$ Required ratio $= x :  rac{x}{4} = 4 : 1$.

questions.question_text: The speed of a railway engine is 42 km/hr when no compartment is attached and the reduction in speed is directly proportional to the square root of the number of compartments attached. If the speed of the train carried by this engine is 24 km/hr with 9 compartments attached, the maximum number of compartments that the engine can pull is
questions.solution:
On attaching 9 compartments to the engine, we have :
reduction in speed $= k	imes rac{	ext{reduction in speed}}{	ext{compartments}} = k	imes rac{9}{1} = 3k$ where $k$ is a constant.
$\therefore 42 - 3k = 24$ or $3k = 18$ or $k = 6$.
For the speed of the engine to be zero, let the number of compartments attached be $x$.
Then, $42 - 6	imes rac{	ext{speed}}{	ext{compartments}} = 0 \Rightarrow 6	imes rac{	ext{speed}}{	ext{compartments}} = 42 \Rightarrow  rac{	ext{speed}}{	ext{compartments}} = 7 \Rightarrow x = 49$.
Hence, maximum number of compartments that the engine can pull $= (49 - 1) = 48$.

questions.passage_text: Ram and Shyam run a race between points A and B, 5 km apart. Ram starts at 9 a.m. from A at a speed of 5 km/hr, reaches B and returns to A at the same speed. Shyam starts at 9 : 45 a.m. from A at a speed of 10 km/hr, reaches B and comes back to A at the same speed.
questions.question_text: At what time do Ram and Shyam meet each other?
questions.solution:
Since $A$ and $B$ are 5 km apart and Ram’s speed is 5 km/hr, so Ram reaches $B$ in 1 hour i.e., at 10 a.m.
Thus, when Ram reaches $B$, Shyam has travelled from 9 : 45 a.m. to 10 a.m. i.e. 15 min.
So, distance covered by Shyam when Ram reaches $B =  rac{15}{60} 	imes 5 =  rac{5}{2}$ km.
Now, Ram starts travelling from $B$ to $A$.
So, relative speed $= (10 + 5)$ km/hr $= 15$ km/hr.
Distance between Ram and Shyam $= 5 -  rac{5}{2} =  rac{5}{2}$ km.
Time taken to cover $ rac{5}{2}$ km at relative speed
$=  rac{5}{2} 	imes  rac{1}{15}$ hr $=  rac{1}{6}$ hr $= 10$ min.
Hence, Ram and Shyam meet each other 10 minutes after 10 a.m. i.e., at 10 : 10 a.m.

questions.passage_text: Ram and Shyam run a race between points A and B, 5 km apart. Ram starts at 9 a.m. from A at a speed of 5 km/hr, reaches B and returns to A at the same speed. Shyam starts at 9 : 45 a.m. from A at a speed of 10 km/hr, reaches B and comes back to A at the same speed.
questions.question_text: At what time does Shyam overtake Ram?
questions.solution:
Time taken by Shyam to reach $B$ from $A =  rac{5}{10}$ hr $=  rac{1}{2}$ hr $= 30$ min.
So, Shyam reaches $B$ at 10 : 15 a.m.
Now, Shyam starts travelling from $B$ to $A$.
Distance between Ram and Shyam $= $ Distance travelled by Ram in 15 min
$=  rac{15}{60} 	imes 5 =  rac{5}{4}$ km.
Relative speed $= (10 - 5)$ km/hr $= 5$ km/hr.
$\therefore$ Time taken to cover $ rac{5}{4}$ km at 5 km/hr
$=  rac{5}{4} 	imes  rac{1}{5}$ hr $=  rac{1}{4}$ hr $= 15$ min.
Hence, Shyam overtakes Ram at 15 min past 10 : 15 a.m. i.e., at 10 : 30 a.m.

questions.question_text: A man can walk up a ‘moving-up’ escalator in 30 seconds. The same man can walk down this ‘moving-up’ escalator in 90 seconds. Assume that his walking speed is same upwards and downwards. How much time will he take to walk up the escalator, when it is not moving?
questions.solution:
Suppose the escalator has $n$ steps.
Let man’s speed be $x$ steps per sec. and the speed of the escalator be $y$ steps per sec.
Then, $x + y =  rac{n}{30}$ and $x - y =  rac{n}{90}$.
Adding, we get : $2x =  rac{4n}{90} =  rac{2n}{45}$ or $x =  rac{n}{45}$.
$\therefore$ Required time $=  rac{n}{(n/45)} = 45$ sec.

questions.question_text: A hare pursued by a hound is 60 of her own leaps before him. When the hare takes 4 leaps, the hound takes 3. In one leap, the hare goes $3\frac{1}{4}$ metres and the hound $3\frac{2}{4}$ metres. In how many leaps will the hound overtake the hare?
questions.solution:
60 leaps of the hare $= 60 	imes  rac{3}{4}$ m $= 105$ m.
So, the hound should gain 105 m over the hare.
When the hound travels $3 	imes  rac{33}{4}$ m $=  rac{99}{4}$ m, the hare travels $4 	imes  rac{7}{4}$ m $= 7$ m.
In 3 leaps of the hound, the hound gains $ rac{33}{4} - 7$ m $=  rac{5}{4}$ m.
$\therefore$ Number of leaps required $= 105 	imes  rac{4}{5} 	imes 3 = 252$.

questions.question_text: Arun had ridden one-third the total distance of his trip when his scooter got punctured. He finished the journey on foot, spending twenty times as long walking as he had spent riding. What was the ratio of his riding speed to his walking speed?
questions.solution:
Let the total distance be $x$ km and time spent in riding be $y$ hours.
Then, distance covered by riding $=  rac{x}{3}$ km.
Distance covered by walking $= (x -  rac{x}{3})$ km $=  rac{2x}{3}$ km.
Time spent in walking $= (20 - y)$ hrs.
Riding speed $=  rac{(x/3)}{y}$ km/hr $=  rac{x}{3y}$ km/hr.
Walking speed $=  rac{(2x/3)}{(20 - y)}$ km/hr $=  rac{2x}{3(20 - y)}$ km/hr.
$\therefore$ Required ratio $=  rac{x}{3y} :  rac{2x}{3(20 - y)} = 1 : 10$.

questions.question_text: A car overtakes a bus travelling from Delhi to Jaipur at 4 : 30 p.m. The car reaches Jaipur at 6 : 00 p.m. After stopping there for 1 hour, it starts back towards Delhi and meets the same bus at 7 : 30 p.m. which was moving towards Jaipur at that time. If both the bus and the car were travelling with uniform speeds on the same route, at what time would the bus reach Jaipur?
questions.solution:
Suppose after meeting the bus, the car travelled $x$ km to reach Jaipur.
Then, it travelled $x$ km in $1 rac{1}{2}$ hours.
Again, it travelled back to meet the bus again in $1 rac{1}{2}$ hours.
Now, distance travelled in 1 hour $=  rac{x}{2/3} =  rac{3x}{2}$ km.
The bus travelled $(x -  rac{x}{3}) =  rac{2x}{3}$ km in 3 hours.
So, it will travel $x$ km in $3 	imes  rac{3}{2x} 	imes x$ hrs $= 4 rac{1}{2}$ hrs.
Hence, the bus will reach Jaipur $4 rac{1}{2}$ hours after 4 : 30 p.m. i.e. at 9 p.m.

questions.question_text: If Karan travels at a speed of 60 kmph and covers a distance in 9 hrs., then how much time will he take to travel the same distance at a speed of 90 kmph?
questions.solution:
Speed of Karan $= 60$ kmph
Time $= 9$ hrs.
Distance $= 	ext{Speed} 	imes 	ext{Time} = 60 	imes 9 = 540$ km.
$\therefore$ Time taken to cover 540 km at 90 km/ph
$=  rac{540}{90} = 6$ hours.

questions.question_text: The speed of a bus is 72 kmph. The distance covered by the bus in 5 sec is
questions.solution:
Speed of bus $= 72$ km/ph $= 72 	imes  rac{5}{18}$ m/sec $= 20$ m/sec.
Let distance covered by bus in 5 sec be $x$
$\therefore$ Distance $= 	ext{Speed} 	imes 	ext{Time}$
$\Rightarrow x = 20 	imes 5 = 100$ meter.

questions.question_text: A man travels for 5 hours 15 minutes. If he covers the first half of the journey at 60km/h and rest at 45km/h. Find the total distance travelled by him.
questions.solution:
Let the distance covered be $2x$ km.
Time $=  rac{	ext{Distance}}{	ext{Speed}}$
Time taken to covers the first half and second half of the journey in $t_1$ and $t_2$ hours
$\Rightarrow  rac{a}{60} = t_1$ ... (i)
$\Rightarrow  rac{a}{45} = t_2$ ... (ii)
Adding (i) and (ii) we get
$t_1 + t_2 =  rac{a}{60} +  rac{a}{45} =  rac{3a + 4a}{180} =  rac{7a}{180}$
$\Rightarrow  rac{7a}{180} =  rac{21}{4}$
$\Rightarrow a =  rac{21 	imes 180}{4 	imes 7} = 135$ km.
$\therefore$ Length of total journey $= 2 	imes 135 = 270$ km.

questions.question_text: Ashok left from place A for place B at 8 a.m. and Rahul left place B for place A at 10.00 a.m. the distance between place A and B is 637 km. If Ashok and Rahul are travelling at a uniform speed of 39kmph and 47 kmph respectively, at what time will they meet?
questions.solution:
Speed of Ashok $= 39$ km/ph
Speed of Rahul $= 47$ km/ph
Distance between place $A$ and $B = 637$ km
Distance covered by Ashok (from 8 am to 10 am) in 2 hours $= 2 	imes 39 = 78$ km
$\therefore$ Remaining distance $= 637 - 78 = 559$
Relative speed $= 39 + 47 = 86$ km/ph
$\therefore$ Time taken to travel 559 km $=  rac{559}{86} = 6.5$ hours
So, they meet at $= (10 	ext{ a.m.} + 6.5)$ hours $= 4:30$ pm.

questions.question_text: A car goes 20 metres in a second. Find its speed in km/hr
questions.solution:
1 m/sec $=  rac{18}{5}$ km/ph
Car cover 20 metres in a second
$\therefore 20$ m/sec $= 20 	imes  rac{18}{5} = 72$ km/ph.

questions.question_text: Two men P and Q start a journey form same place at a speed of 3 km/hr and $3\frac{1}{2}$ km/hr respectively. If they move in the same direction then what is the distance between them after 4 hours?
questions.solution:
Distance between the Speed of $P$ and $Q$
$= 3 rac{1}{2} - 3 =  rac{7}{2} - 3 =  rac{1}{2}$ km/hr.
So, Distance $= 	ext{Speed} 	imes 	ext{Time}$
Time $= 4$ hours
$\Rightarrow D =  rac{1}{2} 	imes 4 = 2$ km.

questions.question_text: Rohan covers $\frac{2}{3}$ rd of a certain distance in 2 hours 30 minutes at the rate of x kmph. He covers the remaining distance at the rate of (x + 2) kmph in 50 minutes. What is the total distance?
questions.solution:
Let the total distance be covered $3a$ km
$2a$ km distance covered in 2 hours 30 minutes at the rate of $k$ km/ph.
According to the question
$	ext{Speed} 	imes 	ext{Time} = 	ext{Distance}$
$x 	imes  rac{30}{60} = 2a$
$\Rightarrow x 	imes  rac{1}{2} = 2a$
$\therefore  rac{x}{2} = 2a$
$\Rightarrow 5x = 4a$ ... (i)
Then, '$a$' km distance covered in 50 minutes at the rate of $(x + 2)$ km/ph.
$(x + 2) 	imes  rac{50}{60} = a$ ... (ii)
On dividing equation (ii) by (i).
$ rac{(x + 2) 	imes 5}{5x} =  rac{6a}{4a}$
$\Rightarrow  rac{x + 2}{x} =  rac{3}{2}$
$\Rightarrow 3x = 2x + 4$
$\Rightarrow x = 4$
From equation (i)
$5 	imes 4 = 4a$
$\Rightarrow a = 5$
$\therefore$ Total distance $= 3a = 3 	imes 5 = 15$ km.

questions.question_text: To reach point B from point A. At 4pm, Sara will have to travel at an average speed of 18 kmph. She will reach point B at 3 pm if she travels at an average speed of 24 kmph. At what average speed should Sara travel to reach point B at 2pm?
questions.solution:
Difference between time $= 1$ hour
Distance between point $AB = x$ km
According to the question
$ rac{x}{18} -  rac{x}{24} = 1$
LCM of 18 and 24 $= 72$
$\Rightarrow  rac{4x - 3x}{72} = 1$
$\Rightarrow x = 72$ km
Time taken at 18 km/ph to cover 72 km
$=  rac{72}{18} = 4$ hours
$\therefore$ Speed to cover 72 km in 2 hours
$=  rac{72}{2} = 36$ km/ph.

questions.question_text: A student goes to school at the rate of $2\frac{1}{2}$ km/h and reaches 6 min late. If he travels at the speed of 3km/h he is 10 min early. What is the distance to the school ?
questions.solution:
Let the distance between school and home be $= D$
According to the given information
$ rac{D}{2 rac{1}{2}} -  rac{D}{3} = 16$ minutes
$\therefore  rac{2D}{5} -  rac{D}{3} =  rac{16}{60}$
$\Rightarrow  rac{6D - 5D}{15} =  rac{16}{60}$
$\Rightarrow D =  rac{16}{60} 	imes 15 = 4$ km.

questions.question_text: Kim and OM are travelling from point A to B, which are 400 km apart, travelling at a certain speed Kim takes one hour more than Om to reach point B. If Kim doubles her speed she will take 1 hour 30 mins less than Om to reach point B. At what speed was Kim driving from point A to B? (In kmph)
questions.solution:
Let the speed of Kim be $a$ and that of Om be $b$.
Distance between point $A$ and $B = 400$ km
Then, $ rac{400}{a} -  rac{400}{b} = 1$
Let, $ rac{1}{a} = x$ and $ rac{1}{b} = y$
$400x - 500y = 1$ ... (i)
Speed of km doubles and she will take lets time i.e. 1 hour 30 minutes than 0 meter
Again, $ rac{400}{b} -  rac{400}{2b} =  rac{3}{2}$
$\therefore 400y - 200x =  rac{3}{2}$
$800y - 400x = 3$
Solving (i) and (ii), we get
$400x - 400y = 1$
$-400x + 800y = 3$
$400y = 4$
$\therefore y =  rac{4}{400} =  rac{1}{100}$ km
$\therefore b = 100$ km
Now, $ rac{400}{a} -  rac{400}{100} = 4$
Or, $ rac{400}{a} = 8$
$\therefore a = 80$ kmph.

questions.question_text: A car covers 650 km in 12 hours and other 850 km in 18 hours. Find the average speed of the car.
questions.solution:
Let distance covered be $x$ in 15 minutes
Average speed = $ rac{	ext{Time distance}}{	ext{Time taken}}$
= $ rac{650+850}{12+18}$ kmph
= $ rac{1500}{30}$ kmph
= 50 kmph

questions.question_text: A vehicle travels at the rate of 80 kmph. What distance will it travel in 15 minutes?
questions.solution:
Speed of vehicle = 80 kmph
= $ rac{80	imes1000}{60}$ m/min.
= $ rac{4000}{3}$ m/min.
$	herefore$ Required distance $x$ = $ rac{4000}{3}	imes15$ = 20000 meter

questions.question_text: Aryan covers a certain distance in 1 hour 30 minutes. He covers two thirds of it at the rate of 4 kmph and remaining distance at the rate of 5 kmph. Find the total distance.
questions.solution:
According to given information
$ rac{2x}{4}+ rac{x}{5}= rac{3}{2}$
$ rac{2x}{12}+ rac{x}{15}= rac{3}{2}$
$ rac{3x}{6}+ rac{x}{15}= rac{3}{2}$
$ rac{5x+2x}{30}= rac{3}{2}$
$ rac{7x}{30}= rac{3}{2}$
$14x=90$
$x= rac{90}{14}=6.4$ km.

questions.question_text: Rani goes to school from her house in 30 minutes. Raja takes 45 minutes in covering the same distance. Find the ratio between time taken by Rani and Raja.
questions.solution:
Rani goes to school from her house = 30 minutes
Raja goes to school from her house = 45 minutes
Required ratio = 30 : 45 = 2 : 3

questions.question_text: The speeds of John and Max are 30 km/h and 40 km/h. Initially Max is at a place L and John is at a place M. The distance between L and M is 650 km. John started his journey 3 hours earlier than Max to meet each other. If they meet each other at a place P somewhere between L and M, then the distance between P and M is:
questions.solution:
Speed of John = 30 km/h
Speed of Max = 40 km/h
Distance between L and M = 650 km
Distance travelled by John in 3 hrs. = $30	imes3=90$ kms
Time taken by Max and John to travel remaining $(650-90=560)$
= $ rac{560}{40+30}=8$ hours.
Distance travelled by John to reach point P = $8	imes30=240$ kms
Distance between P and M = $240+90=330$ kms

questions.question_text: A car travels the first one third of a certain distance with a speed of 10 km/hr, the next one third distance with a speed of 20 km/hr and the last one third distance with a speed of 60 km/hr. The average speed of the car for the whole journey is
questions.solution:
Let the distance travelled by a car be $x$ km.
First $ rac{x}{3}$ km distance cover at speed of 10 km/hr
Second $ rac{x}{3}$ km distance cover at speed = 20 km/hr
Third $ rac{x}{3}$ km distance cover at speed = 60 km/hr
According to given information

questions.question_text: With a uniform speed, a car covers a distance in 8 hours. Had the speed been increased by 4 km/hr, the same distance could have been covered in 7 hours and 30 minutes. What is the distance covered?

According to given information

questions.question_text: A thief is noticed by a policeman from a distance of 200 m. The thief starts running and the policeman chases him. The thief and the policeman run at the speed of 10km/hr and 11 km/hr respectively. What is the distance between them after 6 minutes?
questions.solution:
Speed of thief = 10 km/hr
Speed of policeman = 11 km/hr
Relative speed of policeman with respect to thief
= $(11-10)$ km/hr = 1 km/hr
Thief is noticed by a policeman from a distance of 200m

Distance between them after 6 minutes
= $200-100=100$ m
